   434→  def restore(sandbox:, snapshot_name:, layers: nil)
   435→    user = sandbox.user
   436→    was_tailscale = sandbox.tailscale?
   437→
   438→    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: snapshot_name)
   439→    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s)
   440→
   441→    # Determine the Docker image to use
   442→    if snap&.docker_image.present?
   443→      image_ref = snap.docker_image
   444→    else
   445→      # Fall back to legacy naming
   446→      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{snapshot_name}"
   447→    end
   448→
   449→    restore_container = requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("container")
   450→    restore_home      = snap&.home_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("home"))
   451→    restore_data      = snap&.data_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("data"))
   452→

   454→    if restore_container

   461→
   462→    begin
   463→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)

   474→    if sandbox.tailscale?
   475→      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   476→    end
   477→
   478→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   479→      begin
   480→        old_container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   481→        old_container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   482→        old_container.delete(force: true)
   483→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   484→        # Already gone
   485→      end
   486→    end
   487→
   488→    # ── Restore home directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   489→    if restore_home
   490→      home_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   491→      begin
   492→        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.home_snapshot, home_target)
   493→      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   494→        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore home snapshot: #{e.message}")
   495→      end
   496→    end
   497→
   498→    # ── Restore data directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   499→    if restore_data
   500→      if snap.data_subdir.present?
   501→        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{snap.data_subdir}".chomp("/")
   502→      else
   503→        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   504→      end
   505→      begin
   506→        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.data_snapshot, data_target)
   507→      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   508→        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore data snapshot: #{e.message}")
   509→      end
   510→    end
   511→
   512→    # ── Recreate container ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   513→    final_image = restore_container ? image_ref : sandbox.image
   514→
   515→    container = Docker::Container.create(
   516→      "name" => sandbox.full_name,
   517→      "Image" => final_image,
   518→      "Hostname" => sandbox.full_name,
   519→      "Env" => container_env(user, sandbox),
   520→      "Labels" => { "sandcastle.sandbox" => "true" },
   521→      "HostConfig" => {
   522→        "Runtime" => container_runtime,
   523→        "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
   524→        "Binds" => volume_binds(user, sandbox),
   525→        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
   526→      },
   527→      "NetworkingConfig" => {
   528→        "EndpointsConfig" => { NETWORK_NAME => {} }
   529→      }
   530→    )
   531→
   532→    container.start
   533→    sandbox.update!(container_id: container.id, image: final_image, status: "running")
   534→
   535→    # Pre-write Traefik routes so they're active immediately after restore.
   536→    TerminalManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
   537→    VncManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox) if sandbox.vnc_enabled?
   538→
   539→    if was_tailscale && user.tailscale_enabled?
   540→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   541→    end
   542→
   543→    sandbox
   544→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   545→    raise Error, "Failed to restore snapshot: #{e.message}"
   546→  end

   548→  # Import legacy Docker-only snapshots as DB records (idempotent).
   549→  def import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   550→    repo_prefix = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
   551→
   552→    Docker::Image.all.each do |img|
   553→      repo_tags = img.info["RepoTags"] || []
   554→      repo_tags.each do |tag|
   555→        repo, tag_name = tag.split(":")
   556→        next unless repo == repo_prefix
   557→        next if tag_name.blank?
   558→        next if Snapshot.exists?(user: user, name: tag_name)
   559→
   560→        Snapshot.create!(
   561→          user: user,
   562→          name: tag_name,
   563→          docker_image: tag,
   564→          docker_size: img.info["Size"],
   565→          source_sandbox: img.info.dig("Comment")&.delete_prefix("sandbox:"),
   566→          created_at: Time.at(img.info["Created"] || Time.current.to_i)
   567→        )
   568→      end
   569→    end
   570→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError, ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
   571→    Rails.logger.warn("Legacy snapshot import failed: #{e.message}")
   572→  end
   573→
   574→  def snapshot_json(snap)
   575→    {
   576→      name: snap.name,
   577→      label: snap.label,
   578→      source_sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
   579→      layers: snap.layers,
   580→      docker_image: snap.docker_image,
   581→      docker_size: snap.docker_size,
   582→      home_size: snap.home_size,
   583→      data_size: snap.data_size,
   584→      total_size: snap.total_size,
   585→      created_at: snap.created_at
   586→    }
   587→  end

   589→  def connect_info(sandbox:)
   590→    user = sandbox.user.name
   591→
   592→    unless sandbox.tailscale?
   593→      raise Error, "SSH access requires Tailscale. Enable Tailscale on your account and connect this sandbox to your tailnet."
   594→    end
   595→
   596→    ts_ip = wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   597→    raise Error, "Tailscale IP not available — is the Tailscale sidecar running?" unless ts_ip.present?
   598→
   599→    {
   600→      host: ts_ip,
   601→      port: 22,
   602→      user: user,
   603→      command: "ssh #{user}@#{ts_ip}",
   604→      tailscale_ip: ts_ip
   605→    }
   606→  end
   607→
   608→  private
   609→
   610→  def wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox:, max_attempts: 30, delay: 0.5)
   611→    # Wait for the sandbox to be provisioned (background job) and Tailscale IP to be assigned
   612→    max_attempts.times do
   613→      sandbox.reload # Refresh from DB to get latest status
   614→
   615→      # If sandbox isn't running yet, keep waiting (provision job in progress)
   616→      if sandbox.status != "running"
   617→        sleep delay
   618→        next
   619→      end
   620→
   621→      # Sandbox is running, try to get Tailscale IP
   622→      ts_ip = TailscaleManager.new.sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   623→      return ts_ip if ts_ip.present?
   624→
   625→      sleep delay
   626→    end
   627→    Rails.logger.warn("Tailscale IP not available for sandbox #{sandbox.id} after #{max_attempts} attempts (status: #{sandbox.status})")
   628→    nil
   629→  end

   631→  def connect_to_network(container)
   632→    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
   633→    network.connect(container.id)
   634→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   635→    Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: network #{NETWORK_NAME} not found, skipping network connection")
   636→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   637→    Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: failed to connect container to #{NETWORK_NAME}: #{e.message}")
   638→  end

   640→  def container_env(user, sandbox)
   641→    env = [
   642→      "SANDCASTLE_USER=#{user.name}",
   643→      "SANDCASTLE_SSH_KEY=#{user.ssh_public_key}"
   644→    ]
   645→    env << "USER_EMAIL=#{user.email_address}" if user.email_address.present?
   646→    env << "USER_FULLNAME=#{user.full_name}" if user.full_name.present?
   647→    env << "GITHUB_USERNAME=#{user.github_username}" if user.github_username.present?
   648→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_ENABLED=#{sandbox.vnc_enabled? ? '1' : '0'}"
   649→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_GEOMETRY=#{sandbox.vnc_geometry}"
   650→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_DEPTH=#{sandbox.vnc_depth}"
   651→    env
   652→  end
   653→
   654→  def container_runtime
   655→    @container_runtime ||= begin
   656→      runtimes = Docker.info["Runtimes"] || {}
   657→      if runtimes.key?("sysbox-runc")
   658→        "sysbox-runc"
   659→      else
   660→        Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: sysbox-runc not available, falling back to runc (Docker-in-Docker will not work inside sandboxes)")
   661→        "runc"
   662→      end
   663→    end
   664→  end
   665→
   666→  # mkdir_p with self-healing: if EACCES, fix the parent dir's ownership
   667→  # via a short-lived Docker container (only way when running as non-root
   668→  # inside a container with Docker socket access).
   669→  def ensure_dir(path)
   670→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(path)
   671→  rescue Errno::EACCES
   672→    docker_chown(File.dirname(path))
   673→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(path)
   674→  end
   675→
   676→  # Ensure a bind-mounted directory is world-writable so the sandbox user
   677→  # (non-root inside the Sysbox container) can write to it.
   678→  def prepare_bind_mount(path)
   679→    stat = File.stat(path)
   680→    return if stat.mode & 0o777 == 0o777
   681→    return if system("/usr/bin/sudo", "-n", "/usr/bin/chmod", "777", path)
   682→    docker_chmod(path, "777")
   683→  rescue Errno::ENOENT
   684→    # directory disappeared — race condition, ignore
   685→  end
   686→
   687→  # Fix ownership and permissions of a host path via a busybox container.
   688→  def docker_chown(path)
   689→    docker_run_fix(path, "sh", "-c", "chown #{Process.uid}:#{Process.gid} /mnt && chmod 755 /mnt")
   690→  end
   691→
   692→  def docker_chmod(path, mode)
   693→    docker_run_fix(path, "chmod", mode, "/mnt")
   694→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   695→    Rails.logger.warn("docker_chmod(#{path}) failed: #{e.message}")
   696→  end
   697→
   698→  def docker_run_fix(host_path, *cmd)
   699→    image = fix_image
   700→    c = Docker::Container.create(
   701→      "Image" => image, "Cmd" => cmd,
   702→      "HostConfig" => { "Binds" => [ "#{host_path}:/mnt" ] }
   703→    )
   704→    c.start
   705→    result = c.wait(30)
   706→    exit_code = result&.dig("StatusCode") || -1
   707→    unless exit_code == 0
   708→      raise Error, "docker_run_fix failed (exit #{exit_code}) for #{host_path}: #{cmd.join(' ')}"
   709→    end
   710→  ensure
   711→    c&.delete(force: true) rescue nil
   712→  end
   713→
   714→  # Pick an image guaranteed to be on this Docker daemon.
   715→  # Prefer busybox (tiny), fall back to alpine, then any local image.
   716→  def fix_image
   717→    %w[busybox:latest alpine:latest].each do |img|
   718→      begin
   719→        return img if Docker::Image.get(img)
   720→      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
   721→        next
   722→      end
   723→    end
   724→    # Last resort: use any image already present
   725→    all = Docker::Image.all
   726→    raise Error, "No local images available for docker_run_fix" if all.empty?
   727→    tags = all.first.info["RepoTags"]
   728→    tags&.first || all.first.id
   729→  end
   730→
   731→  def volume_binds(user, sandbox)
   732→    binds = []
   733→    if sandbox.mount_home
   734→      binds << "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home:/home/#{user.name}"
   735→    end
   736→    if sandbox.persistent_volume && sandbox.volume_path
   737→      binds << "#{sandbox.volume_path}:/workspace"
   738→    end
   739→    if sandbox.data_path.present?
   740→      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   741→      binds << "#{host_path}:/data"
   742→    end
   743→    # Chrome profile persistence: mount separately if not mounting full home
   744→    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
   745→      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
   746→      binds << "#{host_path}:/home/#{user.name}/.config/google-chrome"
   747→    end
   748→    binds
   749→  end
   750→end

     5→
     6→  def self.instance

     9→
    10→  # --- Class-level accessors with ENV fallback ---
    11→
    12→  %i[github_client_id google_client_id].each do |attr|
    13→    define_singleton_method(attr) do
    14→      val = instance.send(attr)
    15→      val.presence || ENV[attr.to_s.upcase]
    16→    end
    17→  end
    18→
    19→  %i[github_client_secret google_client_secret].each do |attr|
    20→    define_singleton_method(attr) do
    21→      val = instance.send(attr)
    22→      val.presence || ENV[attr.to_s.upcase]
    23→    end
    24→  end
    25→
    26→  def self.github_configured?
    27→    github_client_id.present? && github_client_secret.present?

    29→
    30→  def self.google_configured?
    31→    google_client_id.present? && google_client_secret.present?
    32→  end

    34→  def self.smtp_configured?

    37→
    38→  def self.smtp_from_address
    39→    val = instance.smtp_from_address
    40→    val.presence || ENV["SMTP_FROM_ADDRESS"] || "noreply@example.com"
    41→  end
    42→
    43→  # Return nil instead of raising on unreadable encrypted values (e.g. after key rotation)
    44→  %i[github_client_secret google_client_secret smtp_password].each do |attr|
    45→    define_method(attr) do
    46→      super()
    47→    rescue ActiveRecord::Encryption::Errors::Decryption
    48→      nil
    49→    end
    50→  end
    51→
    52→  # Skip blank secret values so "leave blank to keep" works
    53→  %i[github_client_secret google_client_secret smtp_password].each do |attr|
    54→    define_method(:"#{attr}=") do |value|
    55→      super(value) if value.present?
    56→    end
    57→  end

    62→    settings = {

    67→      authentication: smtp_authentication.presence&.to_sym,
    68→      enable_starttls_auto: smtp_starttls

    26→    def create
    27→      authorize Sandbox

    29→      manager = SandboxManager.new
    30→
    31→      # Resolve snapshot for container image
    32→      from_snapshot_name = params[:from_snapshot].presence || params[:snapshot].presence
    33→      restore_layers     = params[:restore_layers].present? ? Array(params[:restore_layers]) : nil
    34→
    35→      image = if from_snapshot_name.present?
    36→        # Try to find DB record first

    38→        snap&.docker_image || "sc-snap-#{current_user.name}:#{from_snapshot_name}"
    39→      else
    40→        params[:image].presence || SandboxManager::DEFAULT_IMAGE
    41→      end
    42→
    43→      # Build sandbox record
    44→      sandbox = current_user.sandboxes.build(
    45→        name: params.require(:name),
    46→        status: "pending",
    47→        image: image,
    48→        persistent_volume: params[:persistent] || false,
    49→        mount_home: params[:mount_home] || false,
    50→        data_path: params[:data_path],
    51→        tailscale: params.fetch(:tailscale) { current_user.tailscale_enabled? },

    53→        vnc_geometry: params[:vnc_geometry] || "1280x900",

    55→        temporary: params[:temporary] || false
    56→      )
    57→
    58→      if sandbox.persistent_volume
    59→        sandbox.volume_path = "#{SandboxManager::DATA_DIR}/sandboxes/#{sandbox.full_name}/vol"
    60→      end
    61→
    62→      sandbox.save!
    63→
    64→      # Restore BTRFS layers from snapshot (if requested and available)
    65→      if from_snapshot_name.present? && snap.present?
    66→        want_home = restore_layers.nil? || restore_layers.include?("home")
    67→        want_data = restore_layers.nil? || restore_layers.include?("data")
    68→
    69→        if want_home && snap.home_snapshot.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
    70→          manager.ensure_mount_dirs(current_user, sandbox)
    71→          home_target = "#{SandboxManager::DATA_DIR}/users/#{current_user.name}/home"
    72→          BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.home_snapshot, home_target) rescue nil
    73→        end
    74→
    75→        if want_data && snap.data_snapshot.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
    76→          data_target = if snap.data_subdir.present? && sandbox.data_path.present?
    77→            "#{SandboxManager::DATA_DIR}/users/#{current_user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{snap.data_subdir}".chomp("/")

    80→          end

    82→        end
    83→      end
    84→
    85→      # Enqueue async job
    86→      SandboxProvisionJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: sandbox.id)
    87→
    88→      # Return immediately with job_status so CLI can poll
    89→      render json: sandbox_json(sandbox), status: :created

   113→      end
   114→
   115→      SandboxStartJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
   116→      render json: sandbox_json(@sandbox.reload)
   117→    end
   118→
   119→    def stop
   120→      if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
   121→        render json: { error: "Operation already in progress" }, status: :conflict
   122→        return
   123→      end
   124→
   125→      SandboxStopJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
   126→      render json: sandbox_json(@sandbox.reload)
   127→    end
   128→
   129→    def logs
   130→      tail = (params[:tail] || 200).to_i.clamp(1, 5000)
   131→      logs = SandboxManager.new.logs(sandbox: @sandbox, tail: tail, timestamps: params[:timestamps] == "true")
   132→      render json: { logs: logs }
   133→    end
   134→
   135→    def connect
   136→      # Allow pending sandboxes to return connect info (they may still be provisioning)
   137→      info = SandboxManager.new.connect_info(sandbox: @sandbox)
   138→      render json: info
   139→    rescue SandboxManager::Error => e
   140→      # If sandbox isn't ready yet, return helpful error
   141→      if @sandbox.status == "pending"
   142→        render json: {
   143→          error: "Sandbox is still being provisioned",
   144→          status: @sandbox.status,
   145→          job_status: @sandbox.job_status
   146→        }, status: :accepted
   147→      else
   148→        raise
   149→      end
   150→    end
   151→
   152→    def snapshot
   153→      layers = params[:layers].present? ? Array(params[:layers]) : nil
   154→      snap = SandboxManager.new.create_snapshot(
   155→        sandbox: @sandbox,
   156→        name: params[:name],
   157→        label: params[:label],
   158→        layers: layers,
   159→        data_subdir: params[:data_subdir]
   160→      )
   161→      render json: SandboxManager.new.snapshot_json(snap), status: :created
   162→    end
   163→
   164→    def restore
   165→      layers = params[:layers].present? ? Array(params[:layers]) : nil
   166→      SandboxManager.new.restore(
   167→        sandbox: @sandbox,
   168→        snapshot_name: params.require(:snapshot),
   169→        layers: layers
   170→      )
   171→      render json: sandbox_json(@sandbox.reload)
   172→    end
   173→
   174→    def archive_restore
   175→      @sandbox.start_job("restoring")
   176→      SandboxRestoreJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
   177→      render json: sandbox_json(@sandbox.reload), status: :accepted
   178→    end
   179→
   180→    def purge
   181→      @sandbox.start_job("destroying")
   182→      SandboxDestroyJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id, archive: false)
   183→      render json: { status: "accepted" }, status: :accepted
   184→    end
   185→
   186→    def tailscale_connect
   187→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: @sandbox)
   188→      render json: sandbox_json(@sandbox.reload)
   189→    end
   190→
   191→    def tailscale_disconnect
   192→      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: @sandbox)
   193→      render json: sandbox_json(@sandbox.reload)
   194→    end
   195→
   196→    private
   197→
   198→    def set_sandbox
   199→      @sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
   200→      authorize @sandbox
   201→    rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
   202→      # Check if sandbox exists but is out of scope (destroyed or wrong user)
   203→      sandbox = Sandbox.find_by(id: params[:id])
   204→      if sandbox.nil?
   205→        raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, "Sandbox with ID #{params[:id]} not found"
   206→      elsif sandbox.status.in?(%w[destroyed archived])
   207→        raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, "Sandbox with ID #{params[:id]} has been #{sandbox.status}"
   208→      elsif sandbox.user_id != current_user.id
   209→        raise Pundit::NotAuthorizedError, "You don't have access to this sandbox"
   210→      else
   211→        # Sandbox exists and belongs to user, but not in policy scope - this shouldn't happen
   212→        Rails.logger.error("Sandbox #{params[:id]} exists (status: #{sandbox.status}, user: #{sandbox.user_id}) but not in policy_scope for user #{current_user.id}")
   213→        raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, "Sandbox with ID #{params[:id]} is not accessible (status: #{sandbox.status})"
   214→      end
   215→    end
   216→
   217→    def set_archived_sandbox
   218→      @sandbox = if current_user.admin?
   219→        Sandbox.archived.find(params[:id])
   220→      else
   221→        current_user.sandboxes.archived.find(params[:id])
   222→      end
   223→      authorize @sandbox, action_name == "purge" ? :purge? : :archive_restore?
   224→    rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
   225→      raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, "Archived sandbox with ID #{params[:id]} not found"
   226→    end
   227→
   228→    def sandbox_json(sandbox)
   229→      json = {
   230→        id: sandbox.id,
   231→        name: sandbox.name,
   232→        full_name: sandbox.full_name,
   233→        status: sandbox.status,
   234→        image: sandbox.image,
   235→        persistent_volume: sandbox.persistent_volume,
   236→        mount_home: sandbox.mount_home,
   237→        data_path: sandbox.data_path,
   238→        temporary: sandbox.temporary,
   239→        tailscale: sandbox.tailscale,
   240→        vnc_enabled: sandbox.vnc_enabled,
   241→        vnc_geometry: sandbox.vnc_geometry,
   242→        vnc_depth: sandbox.vnc_depth,
   243→        routes: sandbox.routes.map { |r| { id: r.id, domain: r.domain, port: r.port, url: r.url } },
   244→        created_at: sandbox.created_at,
   245→        archived_at: sandbox.archived_at,
   246→        connect_command: sandbox.connect_command,
   247→        job_status: sandbox.job_status,
   248→        job_error: sandbox.job_error
   249→      }
   250→
   251→      if sandbox.tailscale?
   252→        json[:tailscale_ip] = TailscaleManager.new.sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   253→      end
   254→
   255→      json
   256→    end
   257→  end
   258→end
   259→

1→class SandboxesController < ApplicationController
     2→  before_action :set_sandbox, only: [ :show, :update, :destroy, :start, :stop, :retry, :logs, :metrics ]
     3→  before_action :set_archived_sandbox, only: [ :archive_restore ]
     4→
     5→  def new
     6→    authorize Sandbox
     7→    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
     8→    @btrfs_available = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
     9→  end
    10→
    11→  def show
    12→    @sandbox_snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    13→                           .select { |s| s[:source_sandbox] == @sandbox.name }
    14→    @routes = @sandbox.routes.order(:created_at)
    15→    @btrfs = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
    16→  end
    17→
    18→  def update
    19→    if @sandbox.update(params.require(:sandbox).permit(:name))
    20→      redirect_to @sandbox, notice: "Sandbox renamed to #{@sandbox.name}."
    21→    else
    22→      @sandbox_snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    23→                             .select { |s| s[:source_sandbox] == @sandbox.name }
    24→      @routes = @sandbox.routes.order(:created_at)
    25→      @btrfs = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
    26→      flash.now[:alert] = @sandbox.errors.full_messages.join(", ")
    27→      render :show, status: :unprocessable_entity
    28→    end
    29→  end
    30→
    31→  def create
    32→    authorize Sandbox
    33→
    34→    from_snapshot_name = params[:snapshot].presence
    35→
    36→    image = if from_snapshot_name.present?
    37→      snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: Current.user, name: from_snapshot_name)
    38→      snap&.docker_image || "sc-snap-#{Current.user.name}:#{from_snapshot_name}"
    39→    else
    40→      params[:image].presence || SandboxManager::DEFAULT_IMAGE
    41→    end
    42→
    43→    # Build sandbox record
    44→    # Note: temporary sandboxes can only be created via CLI
    45→    sandbox = Current.user.sandboxes.build(
    46→      name: params.require(:name),
    47→      status: "pending",
    48→      image: image,
    49→      persistent_volume: params[:persistent] == "1",
    50→      mount_home: params[:mount_home] == "1",
    51→      data_path: params[:data_path].presence,
    52→      tailscale: params[:tailscale] == "1",
    53→      vnc_enabled: params[:vnc_enabled] != "0",
    54→      vnc_geometry: Sandbox::VNC_GEOMETRIES.include?(params[:vnc_geometry]) ? params[:vnc_geometry] : "1280x900",
    55→      vnc_depth: Sandbox::VNC_DEPTHS.include?(params[:vnc_depth].to_i) ? params[:vnc_depth].to_i : 24,
    56→      temporary: false
    57→    )
    58→
    59→    if sandbox.persistent_volume
    60→      sandbox.volume_path = "#{SandboxManager::DATA_DIR}/sandboxes/#{sandbox.full_name}/vol"
    61→    end
    62→
    63→    if sandbox.save
    64→      # Enqueue async job
    65→      SandboxProvisionJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: sandbox.id)
    66→
    67→      # Optimistic redirect - dashboard will update via Turbo
    68→      redirect_to root_path, notice: "Creating sandcastle #{sandbox.name}..."
    69→    else
    70→      @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    71→      flash.now[:alert] = "Failed to create sandbox: #{sandbox.errors.full_messages.join(', ')}"
    72→      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    73→    end
    74→  rescue => e
    75→    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    76→    flash.now[:alert] = "Failed to create sandbox: #{e.message}"
    77→    render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    78→  end
    79→
    80→  def metrics
    81→    points = @sandbox.container_metrics.recent
    82→      .map { |m| { t: m.recorded_at.to_i, cpu: m.cpu_percent, mem: m.memory_mb.round(0) } }
    83→
    84→    # Append current live stats so graphs render instantly on page load
    85→    if @sandbox.status == "running" && @sandbox.container_id.present?
    86→      full = Rails.cache.fetch("sandbox_stats_full:#{@sandbox.id}", expires_in: 4.seconds) do
    87→        raw = Docker::Container.get(@sandbox.container_id).stats(stream: false)
    88→        next nil if raw.blank?
    89→        {
    90→          cpu_percent: StatsCalculator.cpu_percent(raw),
    91→          memory_mb: StatsCalculator.memory_mb(raw),
    92→          memory_limit_mb: (raw.dig("memory_stats", "limit") || 0) / 1_048_576.0,
    93→          net_rx: 0, net_tx: 0, disk_read: 0, disk_write: 0, pids: 0
    94→        }
    95→      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    96→        nil
    97→      end
    98→      points << { t: Time.current.to_i, cpu: full[:cpu_percent], mem: full[:memory_mb].round(0) } if full
    99→    end
   100→
   101→    render json: points
   102→  end
   103→
   104→  def logs
   105→    tail = (params[:tail] || 200).to_i.clamp(1, 5000)
   106→    @tail = tail
   107→    @logs = SandboxManager.new.logs(sandbox: @sandbox, tail: tail, timestamps: true)
   108→  rescue SandboxManager::Error => e
   109→    @logs = nil
   110→    @log_error = e.message
   111→  end
   112→
   113→  def destroy
   114→    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
   115→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
   116→      return
   117→    end
   118→
   119→    user = Current.user
   120→    archive = user.effective_archive_retention_days > 0
   121→
   122→    @sandbox.start_job("destroying")
   123→    SandboxDestroyJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id, archive: archive)
   124→
   125→    notice = archive ? "Archiving sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." : "Destroying sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..."
   126→    respond_to do |format|
   127→      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: notice }
   128→      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
   129→    end
   130→  end
   131→
   132→  def archive_restore
   133→    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
   134→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
   135→      return
   136→    end
   137→
   138→    @sandbox.start_job("restoring")
   139→    SandboxRestoreJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
   140→    redirect_to root_path, notice: "Restoring sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..."
   141→  end
   142→
   143→  def start
   144→    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
   145→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
   146→      return
   147→    end
   148→
   149→    @sandbox.start_job("starting")
   150→    SandboxStartJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
   151→
   152→    respond_to do |format|
   153→      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Starting sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
   154→      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
   155→    end
   156→  end
   157→
   158→  def stop
   159→    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
   160→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
   161→      return
   162→    end
   163→
   164→    @sandbox.start_job("stopping")
   165→    SandboxStopJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
   166→
   167→    respond_to do |format|
   168→      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Stopping sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
   169→      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
   170→    end
   171→  end
   172→
   173→  def retry
   174→    return unless @sandbox.job_failed?
   175→
   176→    @sandbox.update!(job_error: nil)
   177→
   178→    case @sandbox.status
   179→    when "destroyed", "pending"
   180→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Cannot retry creation. Please create a new sandbox."
   181→    when "stopped"
   182→      SandboxStartJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
   183→      redirect_to root_path, notice: "Retrying start..."
   184→    when "running"
   185→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Sandbox is already running"
   186→    end
   187→  end
   188→
   189→  private
   190→
   191→  def set_sandbox
   192→    @sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
   193→    authorize @sandbox
   194→  end
   195→
   196→  def set_archived_sandbox
   197→    @sandbox = Current.user.sandboxes.archived.find(params[:id])
   198→    authorize @sandbox, :archive_restore?
   199→  end
   200→end

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a summary document of all this information:

## Summary: Sandbox Creation and Settings in Sandcastle

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's a complete picture of how sandbox creation and settings work:

---

### 1. SANDBOX MODEL & SCHEMA

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/models/sandbox.rb`

**Columns (from migrations):**
- `user_id` - Foreign key to User
- `name` - Sandbox name (lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens/underscores, 63 chars max)
- `container_id` - Docker container ID (null before provisioned)
- `image` - Container image (default: `ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest`)
- `status` - One of: `pending`, `running`, `stopped`, `destroyed`, `archived`
- `ssh_port` - Unique host SSH port (2201-2299 range)
- `persistent_volume` - Boolean, default false
- `volume_path` - Path to persistent volume (if enabled)
- `mount_home` - Boolean, mount persistent home directory
- `data_path` - Optional path to user data directory to mount at `/data`
- `tailscale` - Boolean, connect to Tailscale network
- `vnc_enabled` - Boolean, start VNC display server (default: true)
- `vnc_geometry` - Screen resolution (e.g., "1280x900", default)
- `vnc_depth` - Color depth in bits (8, 16, 24, or 32; default: 24)
- `temporary` - Boolean, auto-remove on exit (default: false)
- `archived_at` - DateTime when archived (null if not archived)
- `job_status` - Current async job status (e.g., "provisioning", "destroying")
- `job_error` - Error message if job fails
- `job_started_at` - Timestamp when job started

**Validations:**
- Name: required, unique per user (excluding destroyed/archived), format: `/\A[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,62}\z/`
- Status: must be in allowed list
- Image: required
- VNC geometry/depth: must be in predefined lists (VNC_GEOMETRIES, VNC_DEPTHS constants)

**Key Methods:**
- `full_name` - Returns `{user.name}-{sandbox.name}`
- `temp?` / `temporary?` - Returns temporary flag
- `job_in_progress?` - Returns true if job_status is set
- `start_job(status)`, `finish_job()`, `fail_job(error)` - Job lifecycle management

---

### 2. SANDBOXMANAGER SERVICE

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/services/sandbox_manager.rb`

**Key Methods:**

#### `create(user:, name:, image:, persistent: false, tailscale: false, mount_home: false, data_path: nil, temporary: false)`
Creates a new sandbox with these steps:
1. Build Sandbox record (not saved yet)
2. Validate the record
3. Create mount directories (home, data, workspace, chrome profile)
4. Save the record
5. Ensure Docker image exists (pull if needed)
6. Create and start the Docker container
7. Optionally connect to Tailscale
8. Returns the Sandbox or raises ServiceName::Error

**Docker Container Configuration:**
- Name: `{user.name}-{sandbox.name}`
- Image: as specified or default
- Runtime: `sysbox-runc` (Docker-in-Docker capable)
- Network: `sandcastle-web`
- Binds: mounts for home, workspace, data, chrome profile as configured
- Restart policy: `unless-stopped`
- Environment variables set:
  - `SANDCASTLE_USER`
  - `SANDCASTLE_SSH_KEY` (user's SSH public key)
  - `USER_EMAIL`, `USER_FULLNAME`, `GITHUB_USERNAME` (if available)
  - `SANDCASTLE_VNC_ENABLED`, `SANDCASTLE_VNC_GEOMETRY`, `SANDCASTLE_VNC_DEPTH`

**Other Key Methods:**
- `destroy(sandbox:, keep_volume: false, archive: false)` - Stop container, optionally archive or delete volume
- `start(sandbox:)` - Restart a stopped sandbox
- `stop(sandbox:)` - Stop a running sandbox
- `restore_from_archive(sandbox:)` - Recreate archived sandbox
- `create_snapshot(sandbox:, name:, label:, layers:, data_subdir:)` - Create snapshots with Docker and BTRFS layers
- `restore(sandbox:, snapshot_name:, layers:)` - Restore from snapshot
- `connect_info(sandbox:)` - Get SSH connection details

---

### 3. USER MODEL & PREFERENCES

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/models/user.rb`

**Columns (from migrations):**
- `email_address` - Unique email
- `password_digest` - bcrypt hash
- `name` - Unique username (lowercase alphanumeric, 2-31 chars)
- `ssh_public_key` - User's SSH public key (injected into sandboxes)
- `admin` - Boolean, admin privileges (default: false)
- `status` - One of: `active`, `suspended`, `pending_approval`
- `full_name` - Display name
- `tailscale_enabled` - Boolean (deprecated; use tailscale_state instead)
- `tailscale_state` - One of: `disabled`, `pending`, `enabled`
- `tailscale_container_id` - ID of Tailscale sidecar container
- `tailscale_network` - Bridge network name for Tailscale
- `tailscale_auto_connect` - Boolean, auto-connect new sandboxes to Tailscale
- `tailscale_subnet` - Subnet assigned to user's Tailscale network
- `chrome_persist_profile` - Boolean, persist Chrome profile across sandboxes (default: true)
- `sandbox_archive_retention_days` - User-level setting for archive retention (nullable, falls back to Setting.instance)
- `terminal_emulator` - `xterm` or `ghostty` (default: `xterm`)

**Key Methods:**
- `tailscale_enabled?` - Returns true if tailscale_state == "enabled"
- `tailscale_auto_connect?` - Returns tailscale_auto_connect value
- `chrome_persist_profile?` - Returns chrome_persist_profile value
- `effective_archive_retention_days` - Falls back to Setting.instance if user setting is nil

---

### 4. SETTING MODEL (GLOBAL DEFAULTS)

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/models/setting.rb`

**Columns (from migrations):**
- `github_client_id`, `github_client_secret` - OAuth configuration
- `google_client_id`, `google_client_secret` - OAuth configuration
- `smtp_address`, `smtp_port`, `smtp_username`, `smtp_password`, `smtp_authentication`, `smtp_starttls`, `smtp_from_address` - Email configuration
- `sandbox_archive_retention_days` - Default archive retention (default: 30), per-user setting can override

**Key Methods:**
- `Setting.instance` - Singleton; returns or creates record with id=1
- `Setting.github_configured?`, `Setting.google_configured?`, `Setting.smtp_configured?` - Check configuration
- `Setting.smtp_from_address` - With ENV fallback
- `smtp_settings` - Returns Rails mailer config hash

**Encrypted Fields:** github_client_secret, google_client_secret, smtp_password (Rails 8 encryption)

---

### 5. API ENDPOINT FOR SANDBOX CREATION

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/controllers/api/sandboxes_controller.rb`

**Endpoint:** `POST /api/sandboxes`

**Request Parameters:**
```
{
  name:              string (required),
  image:             string (optional, default: SandboxManager::DEFAULT_IMAGE),
  persistent:        boolean (optional, default: false),
  mount_home:        boolean (optional, default: false),
  data_path:         string (optional),
  tailscale:         boolean (optional, default: current_user.tailscale_enabled?),
  vnc_enabled:       boolean (optional, default: true),
  vnc_geometry:      string (optional, default: "1280x900"),
  vnc_depth:         integer (optional, default: 24),
  temporary:         boolean (optional, default: false),
  from_snapshot:     string (optional, snapshot name),
  snapshot:          string (optional, legacy alias for from_snapshot),
  restore_layers:    array of strings (optional: "container", "home", "data")
}
```

**Response:** Returns sandbox JSON with all fields including `job_status` (for polling async creation)

**Process:**
1. Build Sandbox record with provided parameters
2. Save the record
3. If restoring from snapshot and BTRFS available, restore home/data layers before container starts
4. Enqueue async job: `SandboxProvisionJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: sandbox.id)`
5. Return immediately with job_status for polling

---

### 6. WEB UI FORM FOR SANDBOX CREATION

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/views/sandboxes/new.html.erb`

**Form Fields:**
- **Basic Information:**
  - Sandbox Name (text input, required, pattern validation)
  - Container Image (text input, optional)
  
- **Restore from Snapshot** (if snapshots exist):
  - Snapshot dropdown (None, or list of available snapshots with labels and layers)
  
- **Storage Options:**
  - Enable persistent volume (checkbox)
  - Mount persistent home directory (checkbox)
  - Data Path (text input for mounting user data to /data)
  
- **Network Options** (if user has Tailscale enabled):
  - Connect to Tailscale network (checkbox, default: checked)
  
- **VNC / Display:**
  - Enable VNC display (checkbox, default: checked)
  - Screen Resolution (dropdown: 1280x900, 1366x768, 1440x900, 1600x900, 1920x1080, 2560x1440)
  - Color Depth (dropdown: 8, 16, 24, 32-bit)

**Features:**
- Auto-generates sandbox name using adjective-noun pattern ("swift-fox", "brave-eagle", etc.)
- Dynamically shows/hides VNC options based on checkbox
- Shows note when BTRFS is unavailable (snapshot container layer only)

---

### 7. CLI SANDBOX CREATION COMMAND

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/vendor/sandcastle-cli/cmd/sandbox.go`

**Command:** `sandcastle create [name]`

**Flags:**
```
--image              Container image (default: ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest)
--persistent         Enable persistent volume
--snapshot           Create from snapshot (legacy)
--from-snapshot      Create from snapshot name
--restore-layers     Comma-separated: container,home,data
--tailscale          Connect to Tailscale
--no-connect (-n)    Don't SSH connect after creation
--rm                 Delete on exit (sets temporary=true)
--home               Mount persistent home
--data [path]        Mount user data directory (or subpath)
--no-vnc             Disable VNC
--vnc-geometry       VNC resolution (e.g., 1920x1080)
--vnc-depth          VNC color depth (8, 16, 24, 32)
```

**Environment Variable Overrides:**
- `SANDCASTLE_HOME=1` - equivalent to `--home`
- `SANDCASTLE_DATA=path` - equivalent to `--data path`
- `SANDCASTLE_RM=1` - equivalent to `--rm`
- `SANDCASTLE_HOME`, `SANDCASTLE_DATA` from config file preferences can be used

**Behavior:**
- If no name provided, auto-generates temp sandbox name: `temp-{unix_timestamp}`
- Auto-generated sandboxes are temporary by default (auto-remove on exit)
- Waits for SSH connection to be ready
- SSH's into sandbox with optional tmux support
- On exit with `--rm`, deletes the sandbox (unless user toggled to "keep")

**Preference Loading** (PreRun hook):
- Reads from CLI config file (`~/.config/sandcastle/config.toml`)
- Priority: explicit flag > environment variable > config preference

---

### 8. CLI API TYPES FOR SANDBOX

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/vendor/sandcastle-cli/api/types.go`

**CreateSandboxRequest:**
```go
{
  Name          string   // required
  Image         string   // optional
  Persistent    bool
  FromSnapshot  string   // snapshot name
  RestoreLayers []string // ["container", "home", "data"]
  Tailscale     bool
  MountHome     bool
  DataPath      string
  Temporary     bool
  VNCEnabled    bool     // default: true
  VNCGeometry   string   // e.g., "1280x900"
  VNCDepth      int      // 8, 16, 24, 32
}
```

**Sandbox Response:**
```go
{
  ID               int       // Unique ID
  Name             string    // Sandbox name
  FullName         string    // "{user}-{name}"
  Status           string    // "pending", "running", "stopped", "destroyed", "archived"
  Image            string    // Container image
  PersistentVolume bool
  MountHome        bool
  DataPath         string
  Temporary        bool
  Tailscale        bool
  TailscaleIP      string    // IP on Tailscale network
  VNCEnabled       bool
  VNCGeometry      string
  VNCDepth         int
  Routes           []SandboxRoute // Reverse proxy routes
  ConnectCommand   string    // "sandcastle connect {name}"
  CreatedAt        time.Time
  ArchivedAt       *time.Time
}
```

---

### 9. SANDBOX CREATION WORKFLOW (COMPLETE FLOW)

**From Web UI:**
1. User fills form in `new.html.erb`
2. POST to `/sandboxes` controller
3. Controller builds Sandbox record and enqueues `SandboxProvisionJob`
4. Returns redirect to dashboard
5. Job runs asynchronously (in background)

**From CLI:**
1. User runs `sandcastle create [name] [flags]`
2. CLI calls API: `POST /api/sandboxes` with CreateSandboxRequest
3. API controller builds record and enqueues job, returns immediately
4. CLI polls `/api/sandboxes/{id}` for job completion (checks `job_status`)
5. Once status is "running", CLI calls `/api/sandboxes/{id}/connect`
6. Waits for SSH port to be ready
7. Exec's SSH into sandbox

**Async Job (`SandboxProvisionJob`):**
1. Validates sandbox record
2. Creates host directories (BTRFS subvolumes or regular dirs)
3. Ensures Docker image is available
4. Creates Docker container with SandboxManager
5. Starts container
6. Updates sandbox.status to "running"
7. Creates Traefik config for terminal/VNC access
8. On error: marks job_error and leaves status as "pending" or "destroyed"

---

### 10. KEY SETTINGS HIERARCHY

**Archive Retention (Days):**
1. Check `user.sandbox_archive_retention_days` (nullable integer)
2. Fall back to `Setting.instance.sandbox_archive_retention_days` (default: 30)
3. If > 0: archiving enabled (soft delete with volume preserved)
4. If 0: full destruction (volume deleted immediately)

**Tailscale Auto-Connect:**
- `user.tailscale_auto_connect` flag
- When true, any new sandbox created with user's Tailscale enabled gets auto-connected

**Terminal Emulator:**
- `user.terminal_emulator` (xterm or ghostty)
- Controls which terminal emulator is used in web UI

**Chrome Profile Persistence:**
- `user.chrome_persist_profile` (default: true)
- When true and sandbox doesn't mount full home, mount `.config/google-chrome` separately

---

### 11. SANDBOX LIFECYCLE STATES

```
pending   → running   → [stopped ↔ running] → destroyed (or archived)
            ↓ (error)
          destroyed
```

- **pending:** Async job in progress, Docker container not yet started
- **running:** Container is running and accessible
- **stopped:** Container exists but is stopped (can be restarted)
- **destroyed:** Container deleted, volume deleted (unless archived)
- **archived:** Soft-delete; container deleted but volume preserved, can be restored

---

This comprehensive overview covers the entire sandbox creation pipeline from model through to API and CLI. The system supports both synchronous (web form with UI feedback) and asynchronous (CLI with polling) creation patterns, with rich configuration options for storage, networking, and display.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a summary document of all this information:

## Summary: Sandbox Creation and Settings in Sandcastle

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's a complete picture of how sandbox creation and settings work:

---

### 1. SANDBOX MODEL & SCHEMA

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/models/sandbox.rb`

**Columns (from migrations):**
- `user_id` - Foreign key to User
- `name` - Sandbox name (lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens/underscores, 63 chars max)
- `container_id` - Docker container ID (null before provisioned)
- `image` - Container image (default: `ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest`)
- `status` - One of: `pending`, `running`, `stopped`, `destroyed`, `archived`
- `ssh_port` - Unique host SSH port (2201-2299 range)
- `persistent_volume` - Boolean, default false
- `volume_path` - Path to persistent volume (if enabled)
- `mount_home` - Boolean, mount persistent home directory
- `data_path` - Optional path to user data directory to mount at `/data`
- `tailscale` - Boolean, connect to Tailscale network
- `vnc_enabled` - Boolean, start VNC display server (default: true)
- `vnc_geometry` - Screen resolution (e.g., "1280x900", default)
- `vnc_depth` - Color depth in bits (8, 16, 24, or 32; default: 24)
- `temporary` - Boolean, auto-remove on exit (default: false)
- `archived_at` - DateTime when archived (null if not archived)
- `job_status` - Current async job status (e.g., "provisioning", "destroying")
- `job_error` - Error message if job fails
- `job_started_at` - Timestamp when job started

**Validations:**
- Name: required, unique per user (excluding destroyed/archived), format: `/\A[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,62}\z/`
- Status: must be in allowed list
- Image: required
- VNC geometry/depth: must be in predefined lists (VNC_GEOMETRIES, VNC_DEPTHS constants)

**Key Methods:**
- `full_name` - Returns `{user.name}-{sandbox.name}`
- `temp?` / `temporary?` - Returns temporary flag
- `job_in_progress?` - Returns true if job_status is set
- `start_job(status)`, `finish_job()`, `fail_job(error)` - Job lifecycle management

---

### 2. SANDBOXMANAGER SERVICE

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/services/sandbox_manager.rb`

**Key Methods:**

#### `create(user:, name:, image:, persistent: false, tailscale: false, mount_home: false, data_path: nil, temporary: false)`
Creates a new sandbox with these steps:
1. Build Sandbox record (not saved yet)
2. Validate the record
3. Create mount directories (home, data, workspace, chrome profile)
4. Save the record
5. Ensure Docker image exists (pull if needed)
6. Create and start the Docker container
7. Optionally connect to Tailscale
8. Returns the Sandbox or raises ServiceName::Error

**Docker Container Configuration:**
- Name: `{user.name}-{sandbox.name}`
- Image: as specified or default
- Runtime: `sysbox-runc` (Docker-in-Docker capable)
- Network: `sandcastle-web`
- Binds: mounts for home, workspace, data, chrome profile as configured
- Restart policy: `unless-stopped`
- Environment variables set:
  - `SANDCASTLE_USER`
  - `SANDCASTLE_SSH_KEY` (user's SSH public key)
  - `USER_EMAIL`, `USER_FULLNAME`, `GITHUB_USERNAME` (if available)
  - `SANDCASTLE_VNC_ENABLED`, `SANDCASTLE_VNC_GEOMETRY`, `SANDCASTLE_VNC_DEPTH`

**Other Key Methods:**
- `destroy(sandbox:, keep_volume: false, archive: false)` - Stop container, optionally archive or delete volume
- `start(sandbox:)` - Restart a stopped sandbox
- `stop(sandbox:)` - Stop a running sandbox
- `restore_from_archive(sandbox:)` - Recreate archived sandbox
- `create_snapshot(sandbox:, name:, label:, layers:, data_subdir:)` - Create snapshots with Docker and BTRFS layers
- `restore(sandbox:, snapshot_name:, layers:)` - Restore from snapshot
- `connect_info(sandbox:)` - Get SSH connection details

---

### 3. USER MODEL & PREFERENCES

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/models/user.rb`

**Columns (from migrations):**
- `email_address` - Unique email
- `password_digest` - bcrypt hash
- `name` - Unique username (lowercase alphanumeric, 2-31 chars)
- `ssh_public_key` - User's SSH public key (injected into sandboxes)
- `admin` - Boolean, admin privileges (default: false)
- `status` - One of: `active`, `suspended`, `pending_approval`
- `full_name` - Display name
- `tailscale_enabled` - Boolean (deprecated; use tailscale_state instead)
- `tailscale_state` - One of: `disabled`, `pending`, `enabled`
- `tailscale_container_id` - ID of Tailscale sidecar container
- `tailscale_network` - Bridge network name for Tailscale
- `tailscale_auto_connect` - Boolean, auto-connect new sandboxes to Tailscale
- `tailscale_subnet` - Subnet assigned to user's Tailscale network
- `chrome_persist_profile` - Boolean, persist Chrome profile across sandboxes (default: true)
- `sandbox_archive_retention_days` - User-level setting for archive retention (nullable, falls back to Setting.instance)
- `terminal_emulator` - `xterm` or `ghostty` (default: `xterm`)

**Key Methods:**
- `tailscale_enabled?` - Returns true if tailscale_state == "enabled"
- `tailscale_auto_connect?` - Returns tailscale_auto_connect value
- `chrome_persist_profile?` - Returns chrome_persist_profile value
- `effective_archive_retention_days` - Falls back to Setting.instance if user setting is nil

---

### 4. SETTING MODEL (GLOBAL DEFAULTS)

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/models/setting.rb`

**Columns (from migrations):**
- `github_client_id`, `github_client_secret` - OAuth configuration
- `google_client_id`, `google_client_secret` - OAuth configuration
- `smtp_address`, `smtp_port`, `smtp_username`, `smtp_password`, `smtp_authentication`, `smtp_starttls`, `smtp_from_address` - Email configuration
- `sandbox_archive_retention_days` - Default archive retention (default: 30), per-user setting can override

**Key Methods:**
- `Setting.instance` - Singleton; returns or creates record with id=1
- `Setting.github_configured?`, `Setting.google_configured?`, `Setting.smtp_configured?` - Check configuration
- `Setting.smtp_from_address` - With ENV fallback
- `smtp_settings` - Returns Rails mailer config hash

**Encrypted Fields:** github_client_secret, google_client_secret, smtp_password (Rails 8 encryption)

---

### 5. API ENDPOINT FOR SANDBOX CREATION

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/controllers/api/sandboxes_controller.rb`

**Endpoint:** `POST /api/sandboxes`

**Request Parameters:**
```
{
  name:              string (required),
  image:             string (optional, default: SandboxManager::DEFAULT_IMAGE),
  persistent:        boolean (optional, default: false),
  mount_home:        boolean (optional, default: false),
  data_path:         string (optional),
  tailscale:         boolean (optional, default: current_user.tailscale_enabled?),
  vnc_enabled:       boolean (optional, default: true),
  vnc_geometry:      string (optional, default: "1280x900"),
  vnc_depth:         integer (optional, default: 24),
  temporary:         boolean (optional, default: false),
  from_snapshot:     string (optional, snapshot name),
  snapshot:          string (optional, legacy alias for from_snapshot),
  restore_layers:    array of strings (optional: "container", "home", "data")
}
```

**Response:** Returns sandbox JSON with all fields including `job_status` (for polling async creation)

**Process:**
1. Build Sandbox record with provided parameters
2. Save the record
3. If restoring from snapshot and BTRFS available, restore home/data layers before container starts
4. Enqueue async job: `SandboxProvisionJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: sandbox.id)`
5. Return immediately with job_status for polling

---

### 6. WEB UI FORM FOR SANDBOX CREATION

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/views/sandboxes/new.html.erb`

**Form Fields:**
- **Basic Information:**
  - Sandbox Name (text input, required, pattern validation)
  - Container Image (text input, optional)
  
- **Restore from Snapshot** (if snapshots exist):
  - Snapshot dropdown (None, or list of available snapshots with labels and layers)
  
- **Storage Options:**
  - Enable persistent volume (checkbox)
  - Mount persistent home directory (checkbox)
  - Data Path (text input for mounting user data to /data)
  
- **Network Options** (if user has Tailscale enabled):
  - Connect to Tailscale network (checkbox, default: checked)
  
- **VNC / Display:**
  - Enable VNC display (checkbox, default: checked)
  - Screen Resolution (dropdown: 1280x900, 1366x768, 1440x900, 1600x900, 1920x1080, 2560x1440)
  - Color Depth (dropdown: 8, 16, 24, 32-bit)

**Features:**
- Auto-generates sandbox name using adjective-noun pattern ("swift-fox", "brave-eagle", etc.)
- Dynamically shows/hides VNC options based on checkbox
- Shows note when BTRFS is unavailable (snapshot container layer only)

---

### 7. CLI SANDBOX CREATION COMMAND

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/vendor/sandcastle-cli/cmd/sandbox.go`

**Command:** `sandcastle create [name]`

**Flags:**
```
--image              Container image (default: ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest)
--persistent         Enable persistent volume
--snapshot           Create from snapshot (legacy)
--from-snapshot      Create from snapshot name
--restore-layers     Comma-separated: container,home,data
--tailscale          Connect to Tailscale
--no-connect (-n)    Don't SSH connect after creation
--rm                 Delete on exit (sets temporary=true)
--home               Mount persistent home
--data [path]        Mount user data directory (or subpath)
--no-vnc             Disable VNC
--vnc-geometry       VNC resolution (e.g., 1920x1080)
--vnc-depth          VNC color depth (8, 16, 24, 32)
```

**Environment Variable Overrides:**
- `SANDCASTLE_HOME=1` - equivalent to `--home`
- `SANDCASTLE_DATA=path` - equivalent to `--data path`
- `SANDCASTLE_RM=1` - equivalent to `--rm`
- `SANDCASTLE_HOME`, `SANDCASTLE_DATA` from config file preferences can be used

**Behavior:**
- If no name provided, auto-generates temp sandbox name: `temp-{unix_timestamp}`
- Auto-generated sandboxes are temporary by default (auto-remove on exit)
- Waits for SSH connection to be ready
- SSH's into sandbox with optional tmux support
- On exit with `--rm`, deletes the sandbox (unless user toggled to "keep")

**Preference Loading** (PreRun hook):
- Reads from CLI config file (`~/.config/sandcastle/config.toml`)
- Priority: explicit flag > environment variable > config preference

---

### 8. CLI API TYPES FOR SANDBOX

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/vendor/sandcastle-cli/api/types.go`

**CreateSandboxRequest:**
```go
{
  Name          string   // required
  Image         string   // optional
  Persistent    bool
  FromSnapshot  string   // snapshot name
  RestoreLayers []string // ["container", "home", "data"]
  Tailscale     bool
  MountHome     bool
  DataPath      string
  Temporary     bool
  VNCEnabled    bool     // default: true
  VNCGeometry   string   // e.g., "1280x900"
  VNCDepth      int      // 8, 16, 24, 32
}
```

**Sandbox Response:**
```go
{
  ID               int       // Unique ID
  Name             string    // Sandbox name
  FullName         string    // "{user}-{name}"
  Status           string    // "pending", "running", "stopped", "destroyed", "archived"
  Image            string    // Container image
  PersistentVolume bool
  MountHome        bool
  DataPath         string
  Temporary        bool
  Tailscale        bool
  TailscaleIP      string    // IP on Tailscale network
  VNCEnabled       bool
  VNCGeometry      string
  VNCDepth         int
  Routes           []SandboxRoute // Reverse proxy routes
  ConnectCommand   string    // "sandcastle connect {name}"
  CreatedAt        time.Time
  ArchivedAt       *time.Time
}
```

---

### 9. SANDBOX CREATION WORKFLOW (COMPLETE FLOW)

**From Web UI:**
1. User fills form in `new.html.erb`
2. POST to `/sandboxes` controller
3. Controller builds Sandbox record and enqueues `SandboxProvisionJob`
4. Returns redirect to dashboard
5. Job runs asynchronously (in background)

**From CLI:**

3. API controller builds record and enqueues job, returns immediately
4. CLI polls `/api/sandboxes/{id}` for job completion (checks `job_status`)
5. Once status is "running", CLI calls `/api/sandboxes/{id}/connect`
6. Waits for SSH port to be ready
7. Exec's SSH into sandbox

**Async Job (`SandboxProvisionJob`):**
1. Validates sandbox record
2. Creates host directories (BTRFS subvolumes or regular dirs)
3. Ensures Docker image is available

5. Starts container
6. Updates sandbox.status to "running"
7. Creates Traefik config for terminal/VNC access
8. On error: marks job_error and leaves status as "pending" or "destroyed"

---

### 10. KEY SETTINGS HIERARCHY

**Archive Retention (Days):**
1. Check `user.sandbox_archive_retention_days` (nullable integer)
2. Fall back to `Setting.instance.sandbox_archive_retention_days` (default: 30)
3. If > 0: archiving enabled (soft delete with volume preserved)
4. If 0: full destruction (volume deleted immediately)

**Tailscale Auto-Connect:**
- `user.tailscale_auto_connect` flag
- When true, any new sandbox created with user's Tailscale enabled gets auto-connected

**Terminal Emulator:**
- `user.terminal_emulator` (xterm or ghostty)
- Controls which terminal emulator is used in web UI

**Chrome Profile Persistence:**
- `user.chrome_persist_profile` (default: true)
- When true and sandbox doesn't mount full home, mount `.config/google-chrome` separately

---

### 11. SANDBOX LIFECYCLE STATES

```
pending   → running   → [stopped ↔ running] → destroyed (or archived)
            ↓ (error)
          destroyed
```

- **pending:** Async job in progress, Docker container not yet started
- **running:** Container is running and accessible
- **stopped:** Container exists but is stopped (can be restarted)
- **destroyed:** Container deleted, volume deleted (unless archived)

1→module Admin
     2→  class SettingsController < BaseController
     3→    def edit
     4→      @setting = Setting.instance
     5→      authorize @setting
     6→    end
     7→
     8→    def update
     9→      @setting = Setting.instance
    10→      authorize @setting
    11→
    12→      # If the user is supplying a new value for an encrypted field, the old
    13→      # (potentially corrupt) DB value must be cleared first — otherwise AR
    14→      # Encryption tries to decrypt it during dirty-tracking and raises.
    15→      corrupt_fields = %i[github_client_secret google_client_secret smtp_password]
    16→                         .select { |f| setting_params[f].present? }
    17→      if corrupt_fields.any?
    18→        @setting.update_columns(corrupt_fields.index_with(nil))
    19→        @setting.reload
    20→      end
    21→
    22→      if @setting.update(setting_params)
    23→        if params[:test_email].present?
    24→          send_test_email
    25→        else
    26→          redirect_to edit_admin_settings_path, notice: "Settings saved."
    27→        end
    28→      else
    29→        render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
    30→      end
    31→    end
    32→
    33→    private
    34→
    35→    def send_test_email
    36→      logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] Starting test email flow"
    37→      logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] Setting.smtp_configured? = #{Setting.smtp_configured?}"
    38→      logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] DB smtp_address = #{Setting.instance.smtp_address.inspect}"
    39→      logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] DB smtp_port = #{Setting.instance.smtp_port.inspect}"
    40→      logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] DB smtp_username = #{Setting.instance.smtp_username.inspect}"
    41→      logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] DB smtp_password present? = #{Setting.instance.smtp_password.present?}"
    42→      logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] DB smtp_from_address = #{Setting.instance.smtp_from_address.inspect}"
    43→      logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] Recipient = #{Current.user.email_address}"
    44→
    45→      if Setting.smtp_configured?
    46→        begin
    47→          logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] Building mail..."
    48→          mail = TestMailer.test(Current.user)
    49→          logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] Mail built: from=#{mail.from.inspect} to=#{mail.to.inspect} delivery_method=#{mail.delivery_method.class}"
    50→          logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] Calling deliver_now..."
    51→          result = mail.deliver_now
    52→          logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] deliver_now returned: #{result.class} message_id=#{result.message_id rescue 'N/A'}"
    53→          redirect_to edit_admin_settings_path, notice: "Settings saved. Test email sent to #{Current.user.email_address}."
    54→        rescue => e
    55→          logger.error "[SMTP-TEST] DELIVERY FAILED: #{e.class}: #{e.message}"
    56→          logger.error "[SMTP-TEST] Backtrace: #{e.backtrace.first(5).join("\n")}"
    57→          redirect_to edit_admin_settings_path, alert: "Settings saved, but failed to send: #{e.class} - #{e.message}"
    58→        end
    59→      else
    60→        logger.warn "[SMTP-TEST] SMTP not configured, skipping send"
    61→        redirect_to edit_admin_settings_path, alert: "Settings saved, but SMTP is not configured. Fill in at least the SMTP address."
    62→      end
    63→    end
    64→
    65→    def setting_params
    66→      params.expect(setting: [
    67→        :github_client_id, :github_client_secret,
    68→        :google_client_id, :google_client_secret,
    69→        :smtp_address, :smtp_port, :smtp_username, :smtp_password,
    70→        :smtp_authentication, :smtp_starttls, :smtp_from_address,
    71→        :sandbox_archive_retention_days
    72→      ])
    73→    end
    74→  end
    75→end

Sandbox Archive

119→

120→ When a sandbox is removed, it is soft-deleted (archived) instead of immediately purged. 121→ Archived sandboxes keep their data on disk and can be restored within the retention window. 122→ Set to 0 to disable archiving (immediate purge). 123→

Default: 30 days. Users can override this in their profile settings.

1→class SandboxesController < ApplicationController
     2→  before_action :set_sandbox, only: [ :show, :update, :destroy, :start, :stop, :retry, :logs, :metrics ]
     3→  before_action :set_archived_sandbox, only: [ :archive_restore ]
     4→
     5→  def new
     6→    authorize Sandbox
     7→    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
     8→    @btrfs_available = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
     9→  end
    10→
    11→  def show
    12→    @sandbox_snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    13→                           .select { |s| s[:source_sandbox] == @sandbox.name }
    14→    @routes = @sandbox.routes.order(:created_at)
    15→    @btrfs = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
    16→  end
    17→
    18→  def update
    19→    if @sandbox.update(params.require(:sandbox).permit(:name))
    20→      redirect_to @sandbox, notice: "Sandbox renamed to #{@sandbox.name}."
    21→    else
    22→      @sandbox_snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    23→                             .select { |s| s[:source_sandbox] == @sandbox.name }
    24→      @routes = @sandbox.routes.order(:created_at)
    25→      @btrfs = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
    26→      flash.now[:alert] = @sandbox.errors.full_messages.join(", ")
    27→      render :show, status: :unprocessable_entity
    28→    end
    29→  end
    30→
    31→  def create
    32→    authorize Sandbox
    33→
    34→    from_snapshot_name = params[:snapshot].presence
    35→
    36→    image = if from_snapshot_name.present?
    37→      snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: Current.user, name: from_snapshot_name)
    38→      snap&.docker_image || "sc-snap-#{Current.user.name}:#{from_snapshot_name}"
    39→    else
    40→      params[:image].presence || SandboxManager::DEFAULT_IMAGE
    41→    end
    42→
    43→    # Build sandbox record
    44→    # Note: temporary sandboxes can only be created via CLI
    45→    sandbox = Current.user.sandboxes.build(
    46→      name: params.require(:name),
    47→      status: "pending",
    48→      image: image,
    49→      persistent_volume: params[:persistent] == "1",
    50→      mount_home: params[:mount_home] == "1",
    51→      data_path: params[:data_path].presence,
    52→      tailscale: params[:tailscale] == "1",
    53→      vnc_enabled: params[:vnc_enabled] != "0",
    54→      vnc_geometry: Sandbox::VNC_GEOMETRIES.include?(params[:vnc_geometry]) ? params[:vnc_geometry] : "1280x900",
    55→      vnc_depth: Sandbox::VNC_DEPTHS.include?(params[:vnc_depth].to_i) ? params[:vnc_depth].to_i : 24,
    56→      temporary: false
    57→    )
    58→
    59→    if sandbox.persistent_volume
    60→      sandbox.volume_path = "#{SandboxManager::DATA_DIR}/sandboxes/#{sandbox.full_name}/vol"
    61→    end
    62→
    63→    if sandbox.save
    64→      # Enqueue async job
    65→      SandboxProvisionJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: sandbox.id)
    66→
    67→      # Optimistic redirect - dashboard will update via Turbo
    68→      redirect_to root_path, notice: "Creating sandcastle #{sandbox.name}..."
    69→    else
    70→      @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    71→      flash.now[:alert] = "Failed to create sandbox: #{sandbox.errors.full_messages.join(', ')}"
    72→      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    73→    end
    74→  rescue => e
    75→    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    76→    flash.now[:alert] = "Failed to create sandbox: #{e.message}"
    77→    render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    78→  end
    79→
    80→  def metrics
    81→    points = @sandbox.container_metrics.recent
    82→      .map { |m| { t: m.recorded_at.to_i, cpu: m.cpu_percent, mem: m.memory_mb.round(0) } }
    83→
    84→    # Append current live stats so graphs render instantly on page load
    85→    if @sandbox.status == "running" && @sandbox.container_id.present?
    86→      full = Rails.cache.fetch("sandbox_stats_full:#{@sandbox.id}", expires_in: 4.seconds) do
    87→        raw = Docker::Container.get(@sandbox.container_id).stats(stream: false)
    88→        next nil if raw.blank?
    89→        {
    90→          cpu_percent: StatsCalculator.cpu_percent(raw),
    91→          memory_mb: StatsCalculator.memory_mb(raw),
    92→          memory_limit_mb: (raw.dig("memory_stats", "limit") || 0) / 1_048_576.0,
    93→          net_rx: 0, net_tx: 0, disk_read: 0, disk_write: 0, pids: 0
    94→        }
    95→      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    96→        nil
    97→      end
    98→      points << { t: Time.current.to_i, cpu: full[:cpu_percent], mem: full[:memory_mb].round(0) } if full
    99→    end
   100→
   101→    render json: points
   102→  end
   103→
   104→  def logs
   105→    tail = (params[:tail] || 200).to_i.clamp(1, 5000)
   106→    @tail = tail
   107→    @logs = SandboxManager.new.logs(sandbox: @sandbox, tail: tail, timestamps: true)
   108→  rescue SandboxManager::Error => e
   109→    @logs = nil
   110→    @log_error = e.message
   111→  end
   112→
   113→  def destroy
   114→    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
   115→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
   116→      return
   117→    end
   118→
   119→    user = Current.user
   120→    archive = user.effective_archive_retention_days > 0
   121→
   122→    @sandbox.start_job("destroying")
   123→    SandboxDestroyJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id, archive: archive)
   124→
   125→    notice = archive ? "Archiving sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." : "Destroying sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..."
   126→    respond_to do |format|
   127→      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: notice }
   128→      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
   129→    end
   130→  end
   131→
   132→  def archive_restore
   133→    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
   134→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
   135→      return
   136→    end
   137→
   138→    @sandbox.start_job("restoring")
   139→    SandboxRestoreJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
   140→    redirect_to root_path, notice: "Restoring sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..."
   141→  end
   142→
   143→  def start
   144→    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
   145→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
   146→      return
   147→    end
   148→
   149→    @sandbox.start_job("starting")
   150→    SandboxStartJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
   151→
   152→    respond_to do |format|
   153→      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Starting sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
   154→      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
   155→    end
   156→  end
   157→
   158→  def stop
   159→    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
   160→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
   161→      return
   162→    end
   163→
   164→    @sandbox.start_job("stopping")
   165→    SandboxStopJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
   166→
   167→    respond_to do |format|
   168→      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Stopping sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
   169→      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
   170→    end
   171→  end
   172→
   173→  def retry
   174→    return unless @sandbox.job_failed?
   175→
   176→    @sandbox.update!(job_error: nil)
   177→
   178→    case @sandbox.status
   179→    when "destroyed", "pending"
   180→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Cannot retry creation. Please create a new sandbox."
   181→    when "stopped"
   182→      SandboxStartJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
   183→      redirect_to root_path, notice: "Retrying start..."
   184→    when "running"
   185→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Sandbox is already running"
   186→    end
   187→  end
   188→
   189→  private
   190→
   191→  def set_sandbox
   192→    @sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
   193→    authorize @sandbox
   194→  end
   195→
   196→  def set_archived_sandbox
   197→    @sandbox = Current.user.sandboxes.archived.find(params[:id])
   198→    authorize @sandbox, :archive_restore?
   199→  end
   200→end
   201→
