1→module Api
     2→  class SandboxesController < BaseController
     3→    before_action :set_sandbox, only: %i[show update destroy start stop logs connect snapshot restore tailscale_connect tailscale_disconnect]
     4→    before_action :set_archived_sandbox, only: %i[archive_restore purge]
     5→
     6→    def index
     7→      authorize Sandbox
     8→      sandboxes = policy_scope(Sandbox)
     9→      render json: sandboxes.map { |s| sandbox_json(s) }
    10→    end
    11→
    12→    def archived_index
    13→      authorize Sandbox
    14→      sandboxes = if current_user.admin?
    15→        Sandbox.archived
    16→      else
    17→        current_user.sandboxes.archived
    18→      end.includes(:user, :routes).order(:name)
    19→      render json: sandboxes.map { |s| sandbox_json(s) }
    20→    end
    21→
    22→    def show
    23→      render json: sandbox_json(@sandbox)
    24→    end
    25→
    26→    def create
    27→      authorize Sandbox
    28→
    29→      manager = SandboxManager.new
    30→
    31→      # Resolve snapshot for container image
    32→      from_snapshot_name = params[:from_snapshot].presence || params[:snapshot].presence
    33→      restore_layers     = params[:restore_layers].present? ? Array(params[:restore_layers]) : nil
    34→
    35→      image = if from_snapshot_name.present?
    36→        # Try to find DB record first
    37→        snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: current_user, name: from_snapshot_name)
    38→        snap&.docker_image || "sc-snap-#{current_user.name}:#{from_snapshot_name}"
    39→      else
    40→        params[:image].presence || SandboxManager::DEFAULT_IMAGE
    41→      end
    42→
    43→      # Build sandbox record
    44→      sandbox = current_user.sandboxes.build(
    45→        name: params.require(:name),
    46→        status: "pending",
    47→        image: image,
    48→        persistent_volume: params[:persistent] || false,
    49→        mount_home: params[:mount_home] || false,
    50→        data_path: params[:data_path],
    51→        tailscale: params.fetch(:tailscale) { current_user.tailscale_enabled? },
    52→        vnc_enabled: params.key?(:vnc_enabled) ? params[:vnc_enabled] : true,
    53→        vnc_geometry: params[:vnc_geometry] || "1280x900",
    54→        vnc_depth: params[:vnc_depth]&.to_i || 24,
    55→        temporary: params[:temporary] || false
    56→      )
    57→
    58→      if sandbox.persistent_volume
    59→        sandbox.volume_path = "#{SandboxManager::DATA_DIR}/sandboxes/#{sandbox.full_name}/vol"
    60→      end
    61→
    62→      sandbox.save!
    63→
    64→      # Restore BTRFS layers from snapshot (if requested and available)
    65→      if from_snapshot_name.present? && snap.present?
    66→        want_home = restore_layers.nil? || restore_layers.include?("home")
    67→        want_data = restore_layers.nil? || restore_layers.include?("data")
    68→
    69→        if want_home && snap.home_snapshot.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
    70→          manager.ensure_mount_dirs(current_user, sandbox)
    71→          home_target = "#{SandboxManager::DATA_DIR}/users/#{current_user.name}/home"
    72→          BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.home_snapshot, home_target) rescue nil
    73→        end
    74→
    75→        if want_data && snap.data_snapshot.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
    76→          data_target = if snap.data_subdir.present? && sandbox.data_path.present?
    77→            "#{SandboxManager::DATA_DIR}/users/#{current_user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{snap.data_subdir}".chomp("/")
    78→          elsif sandbox.data_path.present?
    79→            "#{SandboxManager::DATA_DIR}/users/#{current_user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
    80→          end
    81→          BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.data_snapshot, data_target) rescue nil if data_target
    82→        end
    83→      end
    84→
    85→      # Enqueue async job
    86→      SandboxProvisionJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: sandbox.id)
    87→
    88→      # Return immediately with job_status so CLI can poll
    89→      render json: sandbox_json(sandbox), status: :created
    90→    end
    91→
    92→    def update
    93→      @sandbox.update!(params.permit(:temporary, :name))
    94→      render json: sandbox_json(@sandbox)
    95→    end
    96→
    97→    def destroy
    98→      if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
    99→        render json: { error: "Operation already in progress" }, status: :conflict
   100→        return
   101→      end
   102→
   103→      archive = @sandbox.user.effective_archive_retention_days > 0
   104→      SandboxDestroyJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id, archive: archive)
   105→
   106→      render json: sandbox_json(@sandbox.reload)
   107→    end
   108→
   109→    def start
   110→      if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
   111→        render json: { error: "Operation already in progress" }, status: :conflict
   112→        return
   113→      end
   114→
   115→      SandboxStartJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
   116→      render json: sandbox_json(@sandbox.reload)
   117→    end
   118→
   119→    def stop
   120→      if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
   121→        render json: { error: "Operation already in progress" }, status: :conflict
   122→        return
   123→      end
   124→
   125→      SandboxStopJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
   126→      render json: sandbox_json(@sandbox.reload)
   127→    end
   128→
   129→    def logs
   130→      tail = (params[:tail] || 200).to_i.clamp(1, 5000)
   131→      logs = SandboxManager.new.logs(sandbox: @sandbox, tail: tail, timestamps: params[:timestamps] == "true")
   132→      render json: { logs: logs }
   133→    end
   134→
   135→    def connect
   136→      # Allow pending sandboxes to return connect info (they may still be provisioning)
   137→      info = SandboxManager.new.connect_info(sandbox: @sandbox)
   138→      render json: info
   139→    rescue SandboxManager::Error => e
   140→      # If sandbox isn't ready yet, return helpful error
   141→      if @sandbox.status == "pending"
   142→        render json: {
   143→          error: "Sandbox is still being provisioned",
   144→          status: @sandbox.status,
   145→          job_status: @sandbox.job_status
   146→        }, status: :accepted
   147→      else
   148→        raise
   149→      end
   150→    end
   151→
   152→    def snapshot
   153→      layers = params[:layers].present? ? Array(params[:layers]) : nil
   154→      snap = SandboxManager.new.create_snapshot(
   155→        sandbox: @sandbox,
   156→        name: params[:name],
   157→        label: params[:label],
   158→        layers: layers,
   159→        data_subdir: params[:data_subdir]
   160→      )
   161→      render json: SandboxManager.new.snapshot_json(snap), status: :created
   162→    end
   163→
   164→    def restore
   165→      layers = params[:layers].present? ? Array(params[:layers]) : nil
   166→      SandboxManager.new.restore(
   167→        sandbox: @sandbox,
   168→        snapshot_name: params.require(:snapshot),
   169→        layers: layers
   170→      )
   171→      render json: sandbox_json(@sandbox.reload)
   172→    end
   173→
   174→    def archive_restore
   175→      @sandbox.start_job("restoring")
   176→      SandboxRestoreJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
   177→      render json: sandbox_json(@sandbox.reload), status: :accepted
   178→    end
   179→
   180→    def purge
   181→      @sandbox.start_job("destroying")
   182→      SandboxDestroyJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id, archive: false)
   183→      render json: { status: "accepted" }, status: :accepted
   184→    end
   185→
   186→    def tailscale_connect
   187→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: @sandbox)
   188→      render json: sandbox_json(@sandbox.reload)
   189→    end
   190→
   191→    def tailscale_disconnect
   192→      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: @sandbox)
   193→      render json: sandbox_json(@sandbox.reload)
   194→    end
   195→
   196→    private
   197→
   198→    def set_sandbox
   199→      @sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
   200→      authorize @sandbox
   201→    rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
   202→      # Check if sandbox exists but is out of scope (destroyed or wrong user)
   203→      sandbox = Sandbox.find_by(id: params[:id])
   204→      if sandbox.nil?
   205→        raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, "Sandbox with ID #{params[:id]} not found"
   206→      elsif sandbox.status.in?(%w[destroyed archived])
   207→        raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, "Sandbox with ID #{params[:id]} has been #{sandbox.status}"
   208→      elsif sandbox.user_id != current_user.id
   209→        raise Pundit::NotAuthorizedError, "You don't have access to this sandbox"
   210→      else
   211→        # Sandbox exists and belongs to user, but not in policy scope - this shouldn't happen
   212→        Rails.logger.error("Sandbox #{params[:id]} exists (status: #{sandbox.status}, user: #{sandbox.user_id}) but not in policy_scope for user #{current_user.id}")
   213→        raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, "Sandbox with ID #{params[:id]} is not accessible (status: #{sandbox.status})"
   214→      end
   215→    end
   216→
   217→    def set_archived_sandbox
   218→      @sandbox = if current_user.admin?
   219→        Sandbox.archived.find(params[:id])
   220→      else
   221→        current_user.sandboxes.archived.find(params[:id])
   222→      end
   223→      authorize @sandbox, action_name == "purge" ? :purge? : :archive_restore?
   224→    rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
   225→      raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, "Archived sandbox with ID #{params[:id]} not found"
   226→    end
   227→
   228→    def sandbox_json(sandbox)
   229→      json = {
   230→        id: sandbox.id,
   231→        name: sandbox.name,
   232→        full_name: sandbox.full_name,
   233→        status: sandbox.status,
   234→        image: sandbox.image,
   235→        persistent_volume: sandbox.persistent_volume,
   236→        mount_home: sandbox.mount_home,
   237→        data_path: sandbox.data_path,
   238→        temporary: sandbox.temporary,
   239→        tailscale: sandbox.tailscale,
   240→        vnc_enabled: sandbox.vnc_enabled,
   241→        vnc_geometry: sandbox.vnc_geometry,
   242→        vnc_depth: sandbox.vnc_depth,
   243→        routes: sandbox.routes.map { |r| { id: r.id, domain: r.domain, port: r.port, url: r.url } },
   244→        created_at: sandbox.created_at,
   245→        archived_at: sandbox.archived_at,
   246→        connect_command: sandbox.connect_command,
   247→        job_status: sandbox.job_status,
   248→        job_error: sandbox.job_error
   249→      }
   250→
   251→      if sandbox.tailscale?
   252→        json[:tailscale_ip] = TailscaleManager.new.sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   253→      end
   254→
   255→      json
   256→    end
   257→  end
   258→end
   259→

1→class SandboxProvisionJob < ApplicationJob
     2→  queue_as :default
     3→
     4→  def perform(sandbox_id:)
     5→    sandbox = Sandbox.find(sandbox_id)
     6→    return if sandbox.status == "running" # Idempotent
     7→
     8→    sandbox.start_job("creating")
     9→    manager = SandboxManager.new
    10→
    11→    begin
    12→      # Directory creation
    13→      manager.ensure_mount_dirs(sandbox.user, sandbox)
    14→
    15→      # Image pull (can be slow)
    16→      manager.ensure_image(sandbox.image)
    17→
    18→      # Container creation and start
    19→      manager.create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: sandbox.user)
    20→
    21→      # Connect to Tailscale if enabled
    22→      if sandbox.tailscale? && sandbox.user.tailscale_enabled?
    23→        TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    24→      end
    25→
    26→      sandbox.update!(status: "running")
    27→      sandbox.finish_job
    28→
    29→    rescue => e
    30→      Rails.logger.error("SandboxProvisionJob failed: #{e.message}\n#{e.backtrace.join("\n")}")
    31→      sandbox.fail_job("Failed to create: #{e.message}")
    32→      sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed")
    33→
    34→      raise # Re-raise for Solid Queue retry
    35→    end
    36→  end
    37→end
    38→

1→class Setting < ApplicationRecord
     2→  encrypts :github_client_secret, :google_client_secret, :smtp_password
     3→
     4→  validates :sandbox_archive_retention_days, numericality: { only_integer: true, greater_than_or_equal_to: 0, allow_nil: true }
     5→
     6→  def self.instance
     7→    find_or_create_by(id: 1)
     8→  end
     9→
    10→  # --- Class-level accessors with ENV fallback ---
    11→
    12→  %i[github_client_id google_client_id].each do |attr|
    13→    define_singleton_method(attr) do
    14→      val = instance.send(attr)
    15→      val.presence || ENV[attr.to_s.upcase]
    16→    end
    17→  end
    18→
    19→  %i[github_client_secret google_client_secret].each do |attr|
    20→    define_singleton_method(attr) do
    21→      val = instance.send(attr)
    22→      val.presence || ENV[attr.to_s.upcase]
    23→    end
    24→  end
    25→
    26→  def self.github_configured?
    27→    github_client_id.present? && github_client_secret.present?
    28→  end
    29→
    30→  def self.google_configured?
    31→    google_client_id.present? && google_client_secret.present?
    32→  end
    33→
    34→  def self.smtp_configured?
    35→    instance.smtp_address.present?
    36→  end
    37→
    38→  def self.smtp_from_address
    39→    val = instance.smtp_from_address
    40→    val.presence || ENV["SMTP_FROM_ADDRESS"] || "noreply@example.com"
    41→  end
    42→
    43→  # Return nil instead of raising on unreadable encrypted values (e.g. after key rotation)
    44→  %i[github_client_secret google_client_secret smtp_password].each do |attr|
    45→    define_method(attr) do
    46→      super()
    47→    rescue ActiveRecord::Encryption::Errors::Decryption
    48→      nil
    49→    end
    50→  end
    51→
    52→  # Skip blank secret values so "leave blank to keep" works
    53→  %i[github_client_secret google_client_secret smtp_password].each do |attr|
    54→    define_method(:"#{attr}=") do |value|
    55→      super(value) if value.present?
    56→    end
    57→  end
    58→
    59→  def smtp_settings
    60→    return {} unless smtp_address.present?
    61→
    62→    settings = {
    63→      address: smtp_address,
    64→      port: smtp_port || 587,
    65→      user_name: smtp_username.presence,
    66→      password: smtp_password.presence,
    67→      authentication: smtp_authentication.presence&.to_sym,
    68→      enable_starttls_auto: smtp_starttls
    69→    }
    70→    settings.compact
    71→  end
    72→end
    73→

Perfect! Now I have read all the requested files. Let me provide a comprehensive summary:

## Summary of Key Files Read

I have successfully read and analyzed all 11 requested file categories. Here's a complete overview:

### 1. Admin Settings Controller & View
**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/controllers/admin/settings_controller.rb`
- Provides settings management for the admin panel
- Handles OAuth (GitHub, Google), SMTP configuration, and sandbox archive retention
- Supports encrypted fields: `github_client_secret`, `google_client_secret`, `smtp_password`
- Can clear corrupt encrypted values before updating
- Has test email functionality with extensive logging

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/views/admin/settings/edit.html.erb`
- Form-based UI for configuring GitHub OAuth, Google OAuth, SMTP, and sandbox archive retention
- Password fields show "leave blank to keep" pattern for encrypted fields
- Displays when features are configured or set via ENV variables
- "Save & Send Test Email" button for SMTP configuration validation

### 2. Web UI Sandboxes Controller
**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/controllers/sandboxes_controller.rb`
- `new` action: loads snapshots and checks BTRFS availability
- `create` action: builds sandbox record with all VNC options, persistent volume, Tailscale, mount_home, data_path settings; enqueues `SandboxProvisionJob` asynchronously
- `show` action: displays sandbox details, snapshots, and routes
- `update` action: handles sandbox renaming
- `destroy` action: soft-deletes (archives) or hard-deletes based on `effective_archive_retention_days`
- `archive_restore` action: restores archived sandboxes
- `start`/`stop` actions: manage sandbox state asynchronously
- `retry` action: retries failed jobs
- `metrics` action: returns CPU and memory graphs via Turbo
- `logs` action: streams container logs

### 3. Sandbox Create Form
**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/views/sandboxes/new.html.erb`
- Creates sandbox with name, image, optional snapshot restore
- Storage options: persistent volume, mount persistent home, data path mounting
- Network options: Tailscale (if user has enabled it)
- VNC/Display options: enable/disable, geometry (6 resolutions), depth (8, 16, 24, 32-bit)
- Auto-generates sandbox names with adjective-noun pattern (e.g., "swift-fox")
- JavaScript toggles VNC options visibility based on checkbox

### 4. API Sandboxes Controller
**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/controllers/api/sandboxes_controller.rb`
- RESTful API endpoints for sandbox CRUD, start/stop, snapshots, archive/restore, Tailscale connect/disconnect
- `create` action: same params as web UI but also handles:
  - `from_snapshot` (v2) and `snapshot` (v1 legacy) params
  - `restore_layers` array to selectively restore BTRFS layers (home/data)
  - BTRFS home and data subvolume restoration if snapshot exists
- Returns JSON with full sandbox state including routes, job_status, job_error
- Handles archived sandboxes via `set_archived_sandbox` with admin privilege check

### 5. SandboxProvisionJob
**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/jobs/sandbox_provision_job.rb`
- Async job that provisions new sandboxes
- Steps: ensure mount dirs → pull image → create + start container → connect Tailscale (if enabled)
- Sets status to "running" on success, "destroyed" on failure
- Calls `finish_job` or `fail_job` to track completion
- Re-raises errors for Solid Queue retry mechanism

### 6. Setting Model
**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/models/setting.rb`
- Singleton model (always ID=1) for system-wide settings
- Encrypted fields: `github_client_secret`, `google_client_secret`, `smtp_password`
- Class methods with ENV fallback: `github_client_id`, `github_client_secret`, `google_client_id`, `google_client_secret`, `smtp_from_address`
- Validation: `sandbox_archive_retention_days` >= 0 or null
- Helper methods: `github_configured?`, `google_configured?`, `smtp_configured?`, `smtp_settings`
- Gracefully handles decryption errors (returns nil instead of raising)
- Skips updating encrypted fields if value is blank (allows "leave blank to keep" UX)

### 7. Recent Migration Files
**CreateSettings (20260212183349):** Creates settings table with OAuth and SMTP columns
**AddArchiveRetentionToSettings (20260302000002):** Adds `sandbox_archive_retention_days` default 30
**AddArchiveRetentionToUsers (20260302000003):** Adds nullable `sandbox_archive_retention_days` to users with check constraint >= 0

### 8. CLI Config/Preferences Loading
**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/vendor/sandcastle-cli/internal/config/config.go`
- Config file: `~/.sandcastle/config.yaml`
- Structure: `Servers` (map of aliases to URL/token/insecure), `CurrentServer`, `Preferences`
- Preferences: `connect_protocol` (ssh|mosh), `use_tmux` (bool), `ssh_extra_args` (string), `mount_home` (bool), `data_path` (string)
- Priority: ENV vars > config file > built-in defaults
- ENV vars: `SANDCASTLE_CONNECT_PROTOCOL`, `SANDCASTLE_USE_TMUX`, `SANDCASTLE_SSH_EXTRA_ARGS`, `SANDCASTLE_HOME` (boolean), `SANDCASTLE_DATA` (path)
- Auto-migrates legacy flat format (single server/token fields)
- `LoadPreferences()`: returns effective preferences with ENV overlays

### 9. CLI Sandbox Create Command
**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/vendor/sandcastle-cli/cmd/sandbox.go`
- `createCmd`: Creates sandbox with flags: `--image`, `--persistent`, `--from-snapshot` (v2) / `--snapshot` (v1 legacy), `--restore-layers` (comma-separated), `--tailscale`, `--no-connect`, `--rm` (auto-delete), `--home`, `--data` (with optional subpath), `--no-vnc`, `--vnc-geometry`, `--vnc-depth`
- PreRun hook: loads config preferences, applies priority: explicit flag > env var > config preference
- Auto-generates temporary sandbox names (`temp-<timestamp>`) if no name provided
- Auto-generates user-friendly names (adjective-noun) for web UI
- Waits for SSH readiness before connecting
- Respects preference for tmux vs. plain SSH
- Supports mosh (alternate protocol)
- Auto-removes temporary sandboxes on exit (unless user changed to "keep" during session)
- Other commands: `list`, `delete`, `start`, `stop`, `use`, `set` (temp/keep), `rename`, `unarchive` (archive restore)

### 10. API Client Method (CreateSandbox)
**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/vendor/sandcastle-cli/api/client.go`
- `CreateSandbox(req CreateSandboxRequest) (*Sandbox, error)`: POSTs to `/api/sandboxes`
- Request marshaled to JSON with Bearer token auth
- Returns full `Sandbox` object with job status
- Other key methods:
  - `ListSandboxes()`, `GetSandbox(id)`, `UpdateSandbox(id)`, `DestroySandbox(id)`
  - `ListArchivedSandboxes()`, `ArchiveRestoreSandbox(id)`
  - `StartSandbox(id)`, `StopSandbox(id)`, `ConnectInfo(id)`
  - Device auth: `RequestDeviceCode()`, `PollDeviceToken()`
  - Snapshots, routes, tokens, Tailscale operations

### 11. API Types
**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/vendor/sandcastle-cli/api/types.go`
- **CreateSandboxRequest**: Fields: `Name`, `Image`, `Persistent`, `Snapshot` (legacy), `FromSnapshot`, `RestoreLayers[]`, `Tailscale`, `MountHome`, `DataPath`, `Temporary`, `VNCEnabled`, `VNCGeometry`, `VNCDepth`
- **Sandbox**: ID, Name, FullName, Status, Image, SSHPort, PersistentVolume, MountHome, DataPath, Temporary, Tailscale, TailscaleIP, VNCEnabled, VNCGeometry, VNCDepth, Routes, ConnectCommand, CreatedAt, ArchivedAt
- **UpdateSandboxRequest**: `Temporary` (ptr bool), `Name` (ptr string)
- Supporting types: ConnectInfo, SandboxRoute, Snapshot, Token, User, SystemStatus, ServerInfo, TailscaleStatus

---

### Key Architectural Patterns Observed:

1. **Three-layer create flow**: Web UI form → sandboxes_controller.create → SandboxProvisionJob (async) → SandboxManager
2. **API mirrors Web UI**: Same fields and parameters supported by both channels
3. **Async provisioning**: Jobs handle container creation, Tailscale setup with proper status tracking
4. **Archive pattern**: Soft-delete (archive) with configurable retention, can restore within window
5. **VNC as first-class feature**: All sandboxes have geometry/depth settings, disabled via flag
6. **Tailscale integration**: Per-user sidecar, auto-connect flag, subnet routing
7. **Config preference hierarchy**: ENV > config file > defaults consistently applied
8. **Snapshot flexibility**: BTRFS layer-selective restore (home/data), container image fallback
9. **Encryption handling**: Settings use ActiveRecord Encryption, graceful decryption error handling
10. **CLI preferences persistence**: YAML config with runtime ENV override

All files have been read in their entirety as requested.

class Setting < ApplicationRecord
  encrypts :github_client_secret, :google_client_secret, :smtp_password

  validates :sandbox_archive_retention_days, numericality: { only_integer: true, greater_than_or_equal_to: 0, allow_nil: true }

  def self.instance
    find_or_create_by(id: 1)
  end

  # --- Class-level accessors with ENV fallback ---

  %i[github_client_id google_client_id].each do |attr|
    define_singleton_method(attr) do
      val = instance.send(attr)
      val.presence || ENV[attr.to_s.upcase]
    end
  end

  %i[github_client_secret google_client_secret].each do |attr|
    define_singleton_method(attr) do
      val = instance.send(attr)
      val.presence || ENV[attr.to_s.upcase]
    end
  end

  def self.github_configured?
    github_client_id.present? && github_client_secret.present?
  end

  def self.google_configured?
    google_client_id.present? && google_client_secret.present?
  end

  def self.smtp_configured?
    instance.smtp_address.present?
  end

  def self.smtp_from_address
    val = instance.smtp_from_address
    val.presence || ENV["SMTP_FROM_ADDRESS"] || "noreply@example.com"
  end

  # Return nil instead of raising on unreadable encrypted values (e.g. after key rotation)
  %i[github_client_secret google_client_secret smtp_password].each do |attr|
    define_method(attr) do
      super()
    rescue ActiveRecord::Encryption::Errors::Decryption
      nil
    end
  end

  # Skip blank secret values so "leave blank to keep" works
  %i[github_client_secret google_client_secret smtp_password].each do |attr|
    define_method(:"#{attr}=") do |value|
      super(value) if value.present?
    end
  end

  def smtp_settings
    return {} unless smtp_address.present?

    settings = {
      address: smtp_address,
      port: smtp_port || 587,
      user_name: smtp_username.presence,
      password: smtp_password.presence,
      authentication: smtp_authentication.presence&.to_sym,
      enable_starttls_auto: smtp_starttls
    }
    settings.compact
  end
end

class Sandbox < ApplicationRecord
  include ActionView::RecordIdentifier  # For dom_id in Turbo broadcasts

  belongs_to :user
  has_many :routes, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :container_metrics, dependent: :delete_all

  VNC_GEOMETRIES = %w[1280x900 1366x768 1440x900 1600x900 1920x1080 2560x1440].freeze
  VNC_DEPTHS = [ 8, 16, 24, 32 ].freeze

  validates :name, presence: true,
    uniqueness: { scope: :user_id, conditions: -> { where.not(status: %w[destroyed archived]) } },
    format: { with: /\A[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,62}\z/, message: "must be lowercase alphanumeric" }
  validates :status, inclusion: { in: %w[pending running stopped destroyed archived] }
  validates :image, presence: true
  validates :vnc_geometry, inclusion: { in: VNC_GEOMETRIES }
  validates :vnc_depth, inclusion: { in: VNC_DEPTHS }

  scope :active, -> { where.not(status: %w[destroyed archived]) }
  scope :archived, -> { where(status: "archived") }
  scope :running, -> { where(status: "running") }

  # Turbo Streams for real-time UI updates
  after_create_commit :broadcast_prepend_to_dashboard
  after_update_commit :broadcast_replace_to_dashboard
  after_destroy_commit :broadcast_remove_from_dashboard

  def full_name
    "#{user.name}-#{name}"
  end

  def connect_command
    "sandcastle connect #{name}"
  end

  def routed?
    routes.loaded? ? routes.any? : routes.exists?
  end

  def temp?
    temporary?
  end

  # Job lifecycle management
  def job_in_progress?
    job_status.present?
  end

  def job_failed?
    job_error.present?
  end

  def start_job(status)
    update!(job_status: status, job_started_at: Time.current, job_error: nil)
  end

  def finish_job
    update!(job_status: nil, job_started_at: nil)
  end

  def fail_job(error_message)
    update!(job_status: nil, job_error: error_message, job_started_at: nil)
  end

  private

  def broadcast_prepend_to_dashboard
    broadcast_prepend_to(
      [ user, "dashboard" ],
      partial: "dashboard/sandbox",
      locals: { sandbox: self },
      target: "sandboxes"
    )
  rescue => e
    Rails.logger.warn("Sandbox#broadcast_prepend: #{e.message}")
  end

  def broadcast_replace_to_dashboard
    # If sandbox is destroyed or archived, remove it from the active dashboard
    if status.in?(%w[destroyed archived])
      broadcast_remove_to([ user, "dashboard" ], target: dom_id(self))
    else
      broadcast_replace_to(
        [ user, "dashboard" ],
        partial: "dashboard/sandbox",
        locals: { sandbox: self },
        target: dom_id(self)
      )
    end
  rescue => e
    Rails.logger.warn("Sandbox#broadcast_replace: #{e.message}")
  end

  def broadcast_remove_from_dashboard
    broadcast_remove_to([ user, "dashboard" ], target: dom_id(self))
  rescue => e
    Rails.logger.warn("Sandbox#broadcast_remove: #{e.message}")
  end
end

module Admin
  class SettingsController < BaseController
    def edit
      @setting = Setting.instance
      authorize @setting
    end

    def update
      @setting = Setting.instance
      authorize @setting

      # If the user is supplying a new value for an encrypted field, the old
      # (potentially corrupt) DB value must be cleared first — otherwise AR
      # Encryption tries to decrypt it during dirty-tracking and raises.
      corrupt_fields = %i[github_client_secret google_client_secret smtp_password]
                         .select { |f| setting_params[f].present? }
      if corrupt_fields.any?
        @setting.update_columns(corrupt_fields.index_with(nil))
        @setting.reload
      end

      if @setting.update(setting_params)
        if params[:test_email].present?
          send_test_email
        else
          redirect_to edit_admin_settings_path, notice: "Settings saved."
        end
      else
        render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
      end
    end

    private

    def send_test_email
      logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] Starting test email flow"
      logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] Setting.smtp_configured? = #{Setting.smtp_configured?}"
      logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] DB smtp_address = #{Setting.instance.smtp_address.inspect}"
      logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] DB smtp_port = #{Setting.instance.smtp_port.inspect}"
      logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] DB smtp_username = #{Setting.instance.smtp_username.inspect}"
      logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] DB smtp_password present? = #{Setting.instance.smtp_password.present?}"
      logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] DB smtp_from_address = #{Setting.instance.smtp_from_address.inspect}"
      logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] Recipient = #{Current.user.email_address}"

      if Setting.smtp_configured?
        begin
          logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] Building mail..."
          mail = TestMailer.test(Current.user)
          logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] Mail built: from=#{mail.from.inspect} to=#{mail.to.inspect} delivery_method=#{mail.delivery_method.class}"
          logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] Calling deliver_now..."
          result = mail.deliver_now
          logger.info "[SMTP-TEST] deliver_now returned: #{result.class} message_id=#{result.message_id rescue 'N/A'}"
          redirect_to edit_admin_settings_path, notice: "Settings saved. Test email sent to #{Current.user.email_address}."
        rescue => e
          logger.error "[SMTP-TEST] DELIVERY FAILED: #{e.class}: #{e.message}"
          logger.error "[SMTP-TEST] Backtrace: #{e.backtrace.first(5).join("\n")}"
          redirect_to edit_admin_settings_path, alert: "Settings saved, but failed to send: #{e.class} - #{e.message}"
        end
      else
        logger.warn "[SMTP-TEST] SMTP not configured, skipping send"
        redirect_to edit_admin_settings_path, alert: "Settings saved, but SMTP is not configured. Fill in at least the SMTP address."
      end
    end

    def setting_params
      params.expect(setting: [
        :github_client_id, :github_client_secret,
        :google_client_id, :google_client_secret,
        :smtp_address, :smtp_port, :smtp_username, :smtp_password,
        :smtp_authentication, :smtp_starttls, :smtp_from_address,
        :sandbox_archive_retention_days
      ])
    end
  end
end

Settings

<%= form_with model: @setting, url: admin_settings_path, method: :patch, class: "space-y-8" do |form| %> <%# === GitHub OAuth === %>

GitHub OAuth

Allow users to sign in with their GitHub account.

<%= form.label :github_client_id, "Client ID", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.text_field :github_client_id, value: @setting.github_client_id, placeholder: ENV["GITHUB_CLIENT_ID"].present? ? "Set via ENV" : "Enter client ID", class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>
<%= form.label :github_client_secret, "Client Secret", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.password_field :github_client_secret, placeholder: @setting.github_client_secret.present? ? "Secret is set (leave blank to keep)" : "Enter client secret", class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>
<% if Setting.github_configured? %>

GitHub OAuth is active.

<% elsif ENV["GITHUB_CLIENT_ID"].present? %>

Currently using ENV variables.

<% end %>
<%# === Google OAuth === %>

Google OAuth

Allow users to sign in with their Google account.

<%= form.label :google_client_id, "Client ID", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.text_field :google_client_id, value: @setting.google_client_id, placeholder: ENV["GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID"].present? ? "Set via ENV" : "Enter client ID", class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>
<%= form.label :google_client_secret, "Client Secret", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.password_field :google_client_secret, placeholder: @setting.google_client_secret.present? ? "Secret is set (leave blank to keep)" : "Enter client secret", class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>
<% if Setting.google_configured? %>

Google OAuth is active.

<% elsif ENV["GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID"].present? %>

Currently using ENV variables.

<% end %>
<%# === SMTP === %>

SMTP / Email

Configure outgoing email for password resets and user invites.

<%= form.label :smtp_address, "SMTP Server", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.text_field :smtp_address, placeholder: "smtp.example.com", class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>
<%= form.label :smtp_port, "Port", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.number_field :smtp_port, class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>
<%= form.label :smtp_username, "Username", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.text_field :smtp_username, placeholder: "user@example.com", class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>
<%= form.label :smtp_password, "Password", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.password_field :smtp_password, placeholder: @setting.smtp_password.present? ? "Password is set (leave blank to keep)" : "Enter password", class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>
<%= form.label :smtp_authentication, "Authentication", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.select :smtp_authentication, [["Plain", "plain"], ["Login", "login"], ["CRAM-MD5", "cram_md5"]], {}, class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>
<%= form.label :smtp_from_address, "From Address", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.email_field :smtp_from_address, placeholder: "noreply@example.com", class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>
<%= form.label :smtp_starttls, class: "inline-flex items-center gap-2 text-sm text-gray-700 cursor-pointer" do %> <%= form.check_box :smtp_starttls, class: "rounded border-gray-300" %> Enable STARTTLS <% end %>
<% if Setting.smtp_configured? %>

SMTP is configured.

<% end %>
<%# === Sandbox Archive === %>

Sandbox Archive

When a sandbox is removed, it is soft-deleted (archived) instead of immediately purged. Archived sandboxes keep their data on disk and can be restored within the retention window. Set to 0 to disable archiving (immediate purge).

<%= form.label :sandbox_archive_retention_days, "Retention period (days)", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.number_field :sandbox_archive_retention_days, min: 0, max: 365, class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>

Default: 30 days. Users can override this in their profile settings.

<%= form.submit "Save Settings", class: "rounded-md px-3.5 py-2.5 bg-blue-600 hover:bg-blue-500 text-white text-sm font-medium cursor-pointer" %>
<% end %>

1→class SandboxManager
     2→  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
     3→  DEFAULT_IMAGE = "ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest"
     4→  NETWORK_NAME = "sandcastle-web"
     5→
     6→  class Error < StandardError; end
     7→
     8→  def create(user:, name:, image: DEFAULT_IMAGE, persistent: false, tailscale: false, mount_home: false, data_path: nil, temporary: false)
     9→    # Build sandbox record (not saved yet)
    10→    sandbox = user.sandboxes.build(
    11→      name: name,
    12→      image: image,
    13→      status: "pending",
    14→      persistent_volume: persistent,
    15→      mount_home: mount_home,
    16→      data_path: data_path,
    17→      temporary: temporary
    18→    )
    19→
    20→    if persistent
    21→      sandbox.volume_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/sandboxes/#{sandbox.full_name}/vol"
    22→    end
    23→
    24→    # Validate before doing expensive operations
    25→    sandbox.validate!
    26→
    27→    # Create directories FIRST (can fail fast before saving record)
    28→    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
    29→
    30→    # Now safe to save
    31→    sandbox.save!
    32→
    33→    # Pull image
    34→    ensure_image(image)
    35→
    36→    # Create and start container
    37→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
    38→
    39→    # Connect to Tailscale if requested
    40→    if (tailscale || user.tailscale_auto_connect?) && user.tailscale_enabled?
    41→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    42→    end
    43→
    44→    sandbox
    45→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    46→    sandbox&.update(status: "destroyed") if sandbox&.persisted?
    47→    raise Error, "Failed to create container: #{e.message}"
    48→  rescue => e
    49→    # If anything fails before save, no DB record is created
    50→    # If anything fails after save, mark as destroyed
    51→    sandbox&.update(status: "destroyed") if sandbox&.persisted?
    52→    raise Error, e.message
    53→  end
    54→
    55→  # Public method for job usage
    56→  def create_container_and_start(sandbox:, user:)
    57→    container = Docker::Container.create(
    58→      "name" => sandbox.full_name,
    59→      "Image" => sandbox.image,
    60→      "Hostname" => sandbox.full_name,
    61→      "Env" => container_env(user, sandbox),
    62→      "Labels" => { "sandcastle.sandbox" => "true" },
    63→      "HostConfig" => {
    64→        "Runtime" => container_runtime,
    65→        "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
    66→        "Binds" => volume_binds(user, sandbox),
    67→        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
    68→      },
    69→      "NetworkingConfig" => {
    70→        "EndpointsConfig" => { NETWORK_NAME => {} }
    71→      }
    72→    )
    73→
    74→    container.start
    75→    container.refresh!
    76→    unless container.json.dig("State", "Running")
    77→      state_error = container.json.dig("State", "Error").presence || container.json.dig("State", "Status")
    78→      container.stop rescue nil
    79→      container.delete(force: true) rescue nil
    80→      raise Error, "Container failed to start: #{state_error}"
    81→    end
    82→    sandbox.update!(container_id: container.id, status: "running")
    83→
    84→    # Pre-write Traefik routes so they're active immediately (no wait on first open).
    85→    TerminalManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
    86→    VncManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox) if sandbox.vnc_enabled?
    87→  end
    88→
    89→  # Public method for job usage
    90→  def ensure_image(image)
    91→    Docker::Image.get(image)
    92→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    93→    raise Error, "Snapshot image #{image} not found locally (snapshots are never pulled from a registry)" if image.start_with?("sc-snap-")
    94→    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => image)
    95→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    96→    raise Error, "Failed to pull image #{image}: #{e.message}"
    97→  end
    98→
    99→  # Public method for job usage
   100→  def ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
   101→    # Create BTRFS subvolume for user directory if on BTRFS
   102→    BtrfsHelper.create_user_subvolume(user.name)
   103→
   104→    # Ensure user base directory is writable (may be root-owned from previous install)
   105→    ensure_dir("#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}")
   106→
   107→    if sandbox.mount_home
   108→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   109→      ensure_dir(dir)
   110→      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   111→    end
   112→    if sandbox.data_path.present?
   113→      # Create BTRFS subvolume for data directory if on BTRFS
   114→      BtrfsHelper.create_user_data_subvolume(user.name, sandbox.data_path)
   115→
   116→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   117→      ensure_dir(dir)
   118→      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   119→    end
   120→    if sandbox.persistent_volume && sandbox.volume_path
   121→      ensure_dir(sandbox.volume_path)
   122→      prepare_bind_mount(sandbox.volume_path)
   123→    end
   124→    # Chrome profile persistence: mount .config/google-chrome separately if not mounting full home
   125→    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
   126→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
   127→      ensure_dir(dir)
   128→      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   129→    end
   130→  rescue Errno::EACCES, Errno::ENOENT => e
   131→    raise Error, "Failed to create mount directories: #{e.message}"
   132→  end
   133→
   134→  def destroy(sandbox:, keep_volume: false, archive: false)
   135→    begin
   136→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   137→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   138→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   139→    end
   140→
   141→    begin
   142→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   143→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   144→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   145→    end
   146→
   147→    RouteManager.new.remove_all_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   148→
   149→    if sandbox.tailscale?
   150→      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   151→    end
   152→
   153→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   154→      begin
   155→        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   156→        container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   157→        container.delete(force: true)
   158→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   159→        # Container already gone
   160→      end
   161→    end
   162→
   163→    if archive
   164→      # Soft-delete: keep volume on disk, mark as archived
   165→      sandbox.update!(status: "archived", container_id: nil, archived_at: Time.current)
   166→    else
   167→      unless keep_volume
   168→        FileUtils.rm_rf(sandbox.volume_path) if sandbox.volume_path.present?
   169→      end
   170→      sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   171→    end
   172→  end
   173→
   174→  # Restore an archived sandbox: recreate the container from the existing volume.
   175→  # The container is started immediately; status is set to "running".
   176→  def restore_from_archive(sandbox:)
   177→    raise Error, "Sandbox is not archived" unless sandbox.status == "archived"
   178→
   179→    user = sandbox.user
   180→
   181→    ensure_image(sandbox.image)
   182→    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
   183→
   184→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
   185→    sandbox.update!(archived_at: nil)
   186→
   187→    if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
   188→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   189→    end
   190→
   191→    sandbox
   192→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   193→    raise Error, "Failed to restore archived sandbox: #{e.message}"
   194→  end
   195→
   196→  def start(sandbox:)
   197→    raise Error, "Sandbox is destroyed" if sandbox.status == "destroyed"
   198→    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "running"
   199→
   200→    user = sandbox.user
   201→
   202→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   203→      begin
   204→        old = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   205→        old.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   206→        old.delete(force: true)
   207→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   208→        # already gone
   209→      end
   210→    end
   211→
   212→    # Reset bind-mount directory permissions before starting the new container.
   213→    # After a previous Sysbox run, dirs may be owned by a remapped UID that
   214→    # Rails can't chmod — ensure_mount_dirs uses sudo to reset to 777.
   215→    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
   216→
   217→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
   218→
   219→    TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
   220→    RouteManager.new.reconnect_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   221→
   222→    sandbox
   223→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   224→    sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   225→    raise Error, "Container not found — sandbox must be recreated"
   226→  end
   227→
   228→  def stop(sandbox:)
   229→    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "stopped"
   230→
   231→    begin
   232→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   233→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   234→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   235→    end
   236→
   237→    begin
   238→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   239→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   240→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   241→    end
   242→
   243→    RouteManager.new.suspend_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   244→
   245→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   246→      begin
   247→        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   248→        container.stop(t: 10)
   249→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   250→        # already gone
   251→      end
   252→    end
   253→
   254→    sandbox.update!(status: "stopped")
   255→    sandbox
   256→  end
   257→
   258→  def logs(sandbox:, tail: 200, timestamps: false)
   259→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   260→
   261→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   262→    container.logs(stdout: true, stderr: true, follow: false, tail: tail, timestamps: timestamps)
   263→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   264→    raise Error, "Container not found"
   265→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   266→    raise Error, "Failed to fetch logs: #{e.message}"
   267→  end
   268→
   269→  def status(sandbox:)
   270→    return { state: "destroyed" } if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   271→
   272→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   273→    info = container.json
   274→    {
   275→      state: info.dig("State", "Status"),
   276→      running: info.dig("State", "Running"),
   277→      started_at: info.dig("State", "StartedAt"),
   278→      pid: info.dig("State", "Pid")
   279→    }
   280→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   281→    { state: "not_found" }
   282→  end
   283→
   284→  # Create a composite snapshot (Docker image + optional BTRFS layers).
   285→  # Returns a Snapshot ActiveRecord object.
   286→  #
   287→  # layers: array of "container", "home", "data", "workspace"
   288→  #   nil means "all available" based on sandbox config
   289→  def create_snapshot(sandbox:, name:, label: nil, layers: nil, data_subdir: nil)
   290→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no running container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   291→
   292→    user = sandbox.user
   293→    name = name.presence || Date.today.iso8601
   294→    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s) || %w[container home data workspace]
   295→
   296→    snap = Snapshot.new(
   297→      user: user,
   298→      name: name,
   299→      label: label,
   300→      source_sandbox: sandbox.name,
   301→      data_subdir: data_subdir
   302→    )
   303→
   304→    # ── Container layer ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   305→    if requested_layers.include?("container")
   306→      repo = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
   307→      container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   308→      image = container.commit(
   309→        repo: repo,
   310→        tag: name,
   311→        comment: "sandbox:#{sandbox.name}"
   312→      )
   313→      snap.docker_image = "#{repo}:#{name}"
   314→      snap.docker_size  = image.info["Size"]
   315→    end
   316→
   317→    # ── Home layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   318→    if requested_layers.include?("home") && sandbox.mount_home? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   319→      home_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   320→      home_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/home"
   321→      if Dir.exist?(home_src)
   322→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(home_src, home_dest)
   323→        snap.home_snapshot = home_dest
   324→        snap.home_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(home_dest)
   325→      end
   326→    end
   327→
   328→    # ── Data layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   329→    if requested_layers.include?("data") && sandbox.data_path.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   330→      if data_subdir.present?
   331→        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{data_subdir}".chomp("/")
   332→      else
   333→        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   334→      end
   335→      data_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/data"
   336→      if Dir.exist?(data_src)
   337→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(data_src, data_dest)
   338→        snap.data_snapshot = data_dest
   339→        snap.data_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(data_dest)
   340→      end
   341→    end
   342→
   343→    # ── Workspace layer (BTRFS only) ─────────────────────────────────────────
   344→    if requested_layers.include?("workspace") && sandbox.persistent_volume? && sandbox.volume_path.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   345→      workspace_src  = sandbox.volume_path
   346→      workspace_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/workspace"
   347→      if Dir.exist?(workspace_src)
   348→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(workspace_src, workspace_dest)
   349→        # Store workspace alongside data_snapshot when no data layer taken
   350→        snap.data_snapshot ||= workspace_dest
   351→        snap.data_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(workspace_dest)
   352→      end
   353→    end
   354→
   355→    snap.save!
   356→    snap
   357→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   358→    raise Error, "Failed to create snapshot: #{e.message}"
   359→  rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   360→    raise Error, "Failed to snapshot filesystem layer: #{e.message}"
   361→  end
   362→
   363→  # Legacy alias kept for backward compatibility (used by existing API endpoint).
   364→  def snapshot(sandbox:, name: nil, **opts)
   365→    snap = create_snapshot(sandbox: sandbox, name: name, layers: %w[container])
   366→    {
   367→      name: snap.name,
   368→      image: snap.docker_image,
   369→      sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
   370→      created_at: snap.created_at
   371→    }
   372→  end
   373→
   374→  def list_snapshots(user:)
   375→    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   376→    Snapshot.where(user: user).order(created_at: :desc).map { |s| snapshot_json(s) }
   377→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   378→    raise Error, "Failed to list snapshots: #{e.message}"
   379→  end
   380→
   381→  def find_snapshot(user:, name:)
   382→    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   383→    Snapshot.find_by!(user: user, name: name)
   384→  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
   385→    raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
   386→  end
   387→
   388→  def destroy_snapshot(user:, name:)
   389→    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: name)
   390→
   391→    if snap
   392→      # Remove Docker image
   393→      if snap.docker_image.present?
   394→        begin
   395→          Docker::Image.get(snap.docker_image).remove
   396→        rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   397→          # Already gone
   398→        rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   399→          raise Error, "Failed to remove Docker image: #{e.message}"
   400→        end
   401→      end
   402→
   403→      # Remove BTRFS snapshots
   404→      if snap.home_snapshot.present?
   405→        begin
   406→          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.home_snapshot)
   407→        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   408→          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete home snapshot #{snap.home_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
   409→        end
   410→      end
   411→
   412→      if snap.data_snapshot.present?
   413→        begin
   414→          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.data_snapshot)
   415→        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   416→          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete data snapshot #{snap.data_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
   417→        end
   418→      end
   419→
   420→      snap.destroy!
   421→    else
   422→      # Legacy: try to find and remove Docker image directly
   423→      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{name}"
   424→      begin
   425→        Docker::Image.get(image_ref).remove
   426→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   427→        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
   428→      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   429→        raise Error, "Failed to destroy snapshot: #{e.message}"
   430→      end
   431→    end
   432→  end
   433→
   434→  def restore(sandbox:, snapshot_name:, layers: nil)
   435→    user = sandbox.user
   436→    was_tailscale = sandbox.tailscale?
   437→
   438→    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: snapshot_name)
   439→    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s)
   440→
   441→    # Determine the Docker image to use
   442→    if snap&.docker_image.present?
   443→      image_ref = snap.docker_image
   444→    else
   445→      # Fall back to legacy naming
   446→      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{snapshot_name}"
   447→    end
   448→
   449→    restore_container = requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("container")
   450→    restore_home      = snap&.home_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("home"))
   451→    restore_data      = snap&.data_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("data"))
   452→
   453→    # Validate the Docker image exists if we need it
   454→    if restore_container
   455→      begin
   456→        Docker::Image.get(image_ref)
   457→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   458→        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{snapshot_name}' not found"
   459→      end
   460→    end
   461→
   462→    begin
   463→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   464→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   465→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   466→    end
   467→
   468→    begin
   469→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   470→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   471→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   472→    end
   473→
   474→    if sandbox.tailscale?
   475→      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   476→    end
   477→
   478→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   479→      begin
   480→        old_container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   481→        old_container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   482→        old_container.delete(force: true)
   483→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   484→        # Already gone
   485→      end
   486→    end
   487→
   488→    # ── Restore home directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   489→    if restore_home
   490→      home_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   491→      begin
   492→        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.home_snapshot, home_target)
   493→      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   494→        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore home snapshot: #{e.message}")
   495→      end
   496→    end
   497→
   498→    # ── Restore data directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   499→    if restore_data
   500→      if snap.data_subdir.present?
   501→        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{snap.data_subdir}".chomp("/")
   502→      else
   503→        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   504→      end
   505→      begin
   506→        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.data_snapshot, data_target)
   507→      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   508→        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore data snapshot: #{e.message}")
   509→      end
   510→    end
   511→
   512→    # ── Recreate container ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   513→    final_image = restore_container ? image_ref : sandbox.image
   514→
   515→    container = Docker::Container.create(
   516→      "name" => sandbox.full_name,
   517→      "Image" => final_image,
   518→      "Hostname" => sandbox.full_name,
   519→      "Env" => container_env(user, sandbox),
   520→      "Labels" => { "sandcastle.sandbox" => "true" },
   521→      "HostConfig" => {
   522→        "Runtime" => container_runtime,
   523→        "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
   524→        "Binds" => volume_binds(user, sandbox),
   525→        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
   526→      },
   527→      "NetworkingConfig" => {
   528→        "EndpointsConfig" => { NETWORK_NAME => {} }
   529→      }
   530→    )
   531→
   532→    container.start
   533→    sandbox.update!(container_id: container.id, image: final_image, status: "running")
   534→
   535→    # Pre-write Traefik routes so they're active immediately after restore.
   536→    TerminalManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
   537→    VncManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox) if sandbox.vnc_enabled?
   538→
   539→    if was_tailscale && user.tailscale_enabled?
   540→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   541→    end
   542→
   543→    sandbox
   544→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   545→    raise Error, "Failed to restore snapshot: #{e.message}"
   546→  end
   547→
   548→  # Import legacy Docker-only snapshots as DB records (idempotent).
   549→  def import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   550→    repo_prefix = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
   551→
   552→    Docker::Image.all.each do |img|
   553→      repo_tags = img.info["RepoTags"] || []
   554→      repo_tags.each do |tag|
   555→        repo, tag_name = tag.split(":")
   556→        next unless repo == repo_prefix
   557→        next if tag_name.blank?
   558→        next if Snapshot.exists?(user: user, name: tag_name)
   559→
   560→        Snapshot.create!(
   561→          user: user,
   562→          name: tag_name,
   563→          docker_image: tag,
   564→          docker_size: img.info["Size"],
   565→          source_sandbox: img.info.dig("Comment")&.delete_prefix("sandbox:"),
   566→          created_at: Time.at(img.info["Created"] || Time.current.to_i)
   567→        )
   568→      end
   569→    end
   570→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError, ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
   571→    Rails.logger.warn("Legacy snapshot import failed: #{e.message}")
   572→  end
   573→
   574→  def snapshot_json(snap)
   575→    {
   576→      name: snap.name,
   577→      label: snap.label,
   578→      source_sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
   579→      layers: snap.layers,
   580→      docker_image: snap.docker_image,
   581→      docker_size: snap.docker_size,
   582→      home_size: snap.home_size,
   583→      data_size: snap.data_size,
   584→      total_size: snap.total_size,
   585→      created_at: snap.created_at
   586→    }
   587→  end
   588→
   589→  def connect_info(sandbox:)
   590→    user = sandbox.user.name
   591→
   592→    unless sandbox.tailscale?
   593→      raise Error, "SSH access requires Tailscale. Enable Tailscale on your account and connect this sandbox to your tailnet."
   594→    end
   595→
   596→    ts_ip = wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   597→    raise Error, "Tailscale IP not available — is the Tailscale sidecar running?" unless ts_ip.present?
   598→
   599→    {
   600→      host: ts_ip,
   601→      port: 22,
   602→      user: user,
   603→      command: "ssh #{user}@#{ts_ip}",
   604→      tailscale_ip: ts_ip
   605→    }
   606→  end
   607→
   608→  private
   609→
   610→  def wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox:, max_attempts: 30, delay: 0.5)
   611→    # Wait for the sandbox to be provisioned (background job) and Tailscale IP to be assigned
   612→    max_attempts.times do
   613→      sandbox.reload # Refresh from DB to get latest status
   614→
   615→      # If sandbox isn't running yet, keep waiting (provision job in progress)
   616→      if sandbox.status != "running"
   617→        sleep delay
   618→        next
   619→      end
   620→
   621→      # Sandbox is running, try to get Tailscale IP
   622→      ts_ip = TailscaleManager.new.sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   623→      return ts_ip if ts_ip.present?
   624→
   625→      sleep delay
   626→    end
   627→    Rails.logger.warn("Tailscale IP not available for sandbox #{sandbox.id} after #{max_attempts} attempts (status: #{sandbox.status})")
   628→    nil
   629→  end
   630→
   631→  def connect_to_network(container)
   632→    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
   633→    network.connect(container.id)
   634→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   635→    Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: network #{NETWORK_NAME} not found, skipping network connection")
   636→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   637→    Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: failed to connect container to #{NETWORK_NAME}: #{e.message}")
   638→  end
   639→
   640→  def container_env(user, sandbox)
   641→    env = [
   642→      "SANDCASTLE_USER=#{user.name}",
   643→      "SANDCASTLE_SSH_KEY=#{user.ssh_public_key}"
   644→    ]
   645→    env << "USER_EMAIL=#{user.email_address}" if user.email_address.present?
   646→    env << "USER_FULLNAME=#{user.full_name}" if user.full_name.present?
   647→    env << "GITHUB_USERNAME=#{user.github_username}" if user.github_username.present?
   648→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_ENABLED=#{sandbox.vnc_enabled? ? '1' : '0'}"
   649→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_GEOMETRY=#{sandbox.vnc_geometry}"
   650→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_DEPTH=#{sandbox.vnc_depth}"
   651→    env
   652→  end
   653→
   654→  def container_runtime
   655→    @container_runtime ||= begin
   656→      runtimes = Docker.info["Runtimes"] || {}
   657→      if runtimes.key?("sysbox-runc")
   658→        "sysbox-runc"
   659→      else
   660→        Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: sysbox-runc not available, falling back to runc (Docker-in-Docker will not work inside sandboxes)")
   661→        "runc"
   662→      end
   663→    end
   664→  end
   665→
   666→  # mkdir_p with self-healing: if EACCES, fix the parent dir's ownership
   667→  # via a short-lived Docker container (only way when running as non-root
   668→  # inside a container with Docker socket access).
   669→  def ensure_dir(path)
   670→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(path)
   671→  rescue Errno::EACCES
   672→    docker_chown(File.dirname(path))
   673→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(path)
   674→  end
   675→
   676→  # Ensure a bind-mounted directory is world-writable so the sandbox user
   677→  # (non-root inside the Sysbox container) can write to it.
   678→  def prepare_bind_mount(path)
   679→    stat = File.stat(path)
   680→    return if stat.mode & 0o777 == 0o777
   681→    return if system("/usr/bin/sudo", "-n", "/usr/bin/chmod", "777", path)
   682→    docker_chmod(path, "777")
   683→  rescue Errno::ENOENT
   684→    # directory disappeared — race condition, ignore
   685→  end
   686→
   687→  # Fix ownership and permissions of a host path via a busybox container.
   688→  def docker_chown(path)
   689→    docker_run_fix(path, "sh", "-c", "chown #{Process.uid}:#{Process.gid} /mnt && chmod 755 /mnt")
   690→  end
   691→
   692→  def docker_chmod(path, mode)
   693→    docker_run_fix(path, "chmod", mode, "/mnt")
   694→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   695→    Rails.logger.warn("docker_chmod(#{path}) failed: #{e.message}")
   696→  end
   697→
   698→  def docker_run_fix(host_path, *cmd)
   699→    image = fix_image
   700→    c = Docker::Container.create(
   701→      "Image" => image, "Cmd" => cmd,
   702→      "HostConfig" => { "Binds" => [ "#{host_path}:/mnt" ] }
   703→    )
   704→    c.start
   705→    result = c.wait(30)
   706→    exit_code = result&.dig("StatusCode") || -1
   707→    unless exit_code == 0
   708→      raise Error, "docker_run_fix failed (exit #{exit_code}) for #{host_path}: #{cmd.join(' ')}"
   709→    end
   710→  ensure
   711→    c&.delete(force: true) rescue nil
   712→  end
   713→
   714→  # Pick an image guaranteed to be on this Docker daemon.
   715→  # Prefer busybox (tiny), fall back to alpine, then any local image.
   716→  def fix_image
   717→    %w[busybox:latest alpine:latest].each do |img|
   718→      begin
   719→        return img if Docker::Image.get(img)
   720→      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
   721→        next
   722→      end
   723→    end
   724→    # Last resort: use any image already present
   725→    all = Docker::Image.all
   726→    raise Error, "No local images available for docker_run_fix" if all.empty?
   727→    tags = all.first.info["RepoTags"]
   728→    tags&.first || all.first.id
   729→  end
   730→
   731→  def volume_binds(user, sandbox)
   732→    binds = []
   733→    if sandbox.mount_home
   734→      binds << "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home:/home/#{user.name}"
   735→    end
   736→    if sandbox.persistent_volume && sandbox.volume_path
   737→      binds << "#{sandbox.volume_path}:/workspace"
   738→    end
   739→    if sandbox.data_path.present?
   740→      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   741→      binds << "#{host_path}:/data"
   742→    end
   743→    # Chrome profile persistence: mount separately if not mounting full home
   744→    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
   745→      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
   746→      binds << "#{host_path}:/home/#{user.name}/.config/google-chrome"
   747→    end
   748→    binds
   749→  end
   750→end
   751→
